words.meaning_en: an advantage or profit gained from something
words.derivation: bene(好的) + fit(做) → 做好事 → 益处
words.example: Exercise has many health benefits.
words.example_zh: 运动有很多健康方面的益处。

words.breakdown: bene-(好的) + fic-(做) + -ial(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: favorable or advantageous; resulting in good
words.derivation: bene(好的) + fic(做) + ial(…的) → 做好事的 → 有益的
words.example: A good diet is beneficial to health.
words.example_zh: 良好的饮食对健康有益。

words.breakdown: bene-(好的) + vol-(意愿) + -ent(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: well-meaning and kindly
words.derivation: bene(好的) + vol(意愿) + ent(…的) → 有好意愿的 → 仁慈的
words.example: The benevolent organization provided food for the homeless.
words.example_zh: 这个慈善组织为无家可归者提供食物。

words.meaning_en: a person who gives money or other help to a person or cause
words.derivation: bene(好的) + fact(做) + or(人) → 做好事的人 → 恩人
words.example: The museum was built thanks to a generous benefactor.
words.example_zh: 这座博物馆的建成多亏了一位慷慨的捐助者。

words.meaning_en: the utterance or bestowing of a blessing
words.derivation: bene(好的) + dict(说) + ion(行为) → 说好话 → 祝福
words.example: The priest offered a benediction at the end of the ceremony.
words.example_zh: 牧师在仪式结束时献上了祝福。

words.meaning_en: a failure to function in a normal or satisfactory manner
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + function(功能) → 功能变坏 → 故障
words.example: A malfunction in the engine caused the plane to land early.
words.example_zh: 发动机故障导致飞机提前降落。

words.meaning_en: intending or intended to do harm
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + icious(充满…的) → 充满坏心的 → 恶意的
words.example: The malicious software damaged thousands of computers.
words.example_zh: 这个恶意软件损坏了数千台电脑。

words.meaning_en: lack of proper nutrition
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + nutrition(营养) → 营养状况不好 → 营养不良
words.example: Malnutrition is a serious problem in developing countries.
words.example_zh: 营养不良是发展中国家的一个严重问题。

words.meaning_en: very virulent or infectious; tending to produce death
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + ignant(具有…性质的) → 具有恶性的 → 恶性的
words.example: The doctor discovered a malignant tumor in the patient.
words.example_zh: 医生在患者体内发现了一个恶性肿瘤。

words.meaning_en: improper, illegal, or negligent professional activity
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + practice(实践) → 不良的执业行为 → 渎职
words.example: The doctor was sued for medical malpractice.
words.example_zh: 这位医生因医疗事故被起诉。

words.meaning_en: a disease or ailment
words.derivation: mal(坏) + ady(状态) → 坏的状态 → 疾病
words.example: Poverty is a social malady that affects millions of people worldwide.
words.example_zh: 贫困是影响全球数百万人的社会弊病。

words.meaning_en: a general feeling of discomfort or uneasiness
words.derivation: mal(坏) + aise(舒适) → 不舒适的感觉 → 不适
words.example: A sense of malaise spread through the organization after the layoffs.
words.example_zh: 裁员后，一种不安的情绪在整个组织中蔓延开来。

words.meaning_en: a person who is dissatisfied and rebellious
words.derivation: mal(坏/不) + content(满意) → 不满意的 → 不满者
words.example: The malcontents in the workforce organized a protest against the new policies.
words.example_zh: 员工中的不满者组织了一场抗议新政策的活动。

words.meaning_en: wrongdoing, especially by a public official
words.derivation: mal(坏) + feas(做) + ance(行为) → 做坏事 → 违法行为
words.example: The mayor was removed from office for malfeasance.
words.example_zh: 市长因渎职行为被免职。

words.meaning_en: to say or estimate that something will happen in the future
words.derivation: pre(提前) + dict(说) → 提前说出 → 预测
words.example: Scientists predict that sea levels will continue to rise.
words.example_zh: 科学家预测海平面将继续上升。

words.meaning_en: to deny the truth of a statement by asserting the opposite
words.derivation: contra(相反) + dict(说) → 反着说 → 反驳
words.example: The new evidence contradicts the previous theory.
words.example_zh: 新证据与之前的理论相矛盾。

words.meaning_en: to state or order authoritatively; to control or influence
words.derivation: dict(说) + ate(做) → 说了就要做 → 命令
words.example: The teacher dictated the notes to the students.
words.example_zh: 老师向学生口述了笔记。

words.meaning_en: a sign or piece of information that indicates something
words.derivation: in(朝向) + dict(说) + ation(行为) → 朝着你说的东西 → 指示
words.example: There is no indication that the economy will improve soon.
words.example_zh: 没有迹象表明经济将很快好转。

words.meaning_en: a decision on a disputed issue in a civil or criminal case
words.derivation: ver(真实) + dict(说) → 说出真相 → 裁决
words.example: The jury reached a verdict of not guilty.
words.example_zh: 陪审团做出了无罪裁决。

words.meaning_en: a difficult, unpleasant, or embarrassing situation
words.derivation: pre(预先) + dic(说) + ament → 预先被宣判的状况 → 困境
words.example: The hikers found themselves in a predicament when the bridge was washed away by the flood.
words.example_zh: 当桥被洪水冲走后，徒步旅行者们发现自己陷入了困境。

words.meaning_en: an official order or proclamation issued by a person in authority
words.derivation: e(出) + dict(说) → 向外宣告 → 法令
words.example: The emperor issued an edict banning the practice throughout the empire.
words.example_zh: 皇帝颁布了一道法令，在全国范围内禁止这一做法。

words.meaning_en: to formally accuse of or charge with a serious crime
words.derivation: in(对) + dict(宣告) → 对某人宣告罪行 → 起诉
words.example: The grand jury voted to indict the former official on corruption charges.
words.example_zh: 大陪审团投票决定以腐败罪起诉这位前官员。

words.breakdown: ab-(离开) + dic-(说，宣告) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to renounce a throne, right, or responsibility formally
words.derivation: ab(离开) + dic(宣告) + ate(使…) → 宣告离开 → 退位
words.example: The king abdicated the throne in favor of his eldest son.
words.example_zh: 国王退位，将王位传给了他的长子。

words.breakdown: vin-(力量) + dict-(说，宣告) + -ive(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: having or showing a strong desire for revenge
words.derivation: vin(力量) + dict(宣告) + ive(…的) → 用力量宣告惩罚 → 报复性的
words.example: Her vindictive ex-partner tried to sabotage her career after the breakup.
words.example_zh: 她那个怀恨在心的前任在分手后试图破坏她的事业。

words.meaning_en: an authoritative prohibition; to prohibit or forbid
words.derivation: inter(在…之间) + dict(命令) → 在中间下命令阻止 → 禁止
words.example: The court issued an interdict preventing the company from demolishing the historic building.
words.example_zh: 法院发出禁令，禁止该公司拆除这座历史建筑。

words.meaning_en: to give an account in words of someone or something
words.derivation: de(完全) + scrib(写) → 完全写下来 → 描述
words.example: Can you describe the painting you saw at the museum?
words.example_zh: 你能描述一下你在博物馆看到的那幅画吗？

words.meaning_en: to advise and authorize the use of a medicine or treatment
words.derivation: pre(提前) + scrib(写) → 提前写好 → 开处方
words.example: The doctor prescribed antibiotics for the infection.
words.example_zh: 医生为感染开了抗生素处方。

words.meaning_en: to arrange to receive something regularly; to express agreement
words.derivation: sub(在下方) + scrib(写) → 在下方签名 → 订阅
words.example: Many students subscribe to academic journals online.
words.example_zh: 许多学生在线订阅学术期刊。

words.meaning_en: a book, document, or piece of music written by hand
words.derivation: manu(手) + script(写) → 用手写的 → 手稿
words.example: The ancient manuscript was discovered in a cave.
words.example_zh: 这份古代手稿是在一个洞穴中发现的。

words.meaning_en: words inscribed, as on a monument or in a book
words.derivation: in(在…上) + script(写) + ion(行为) → 刻写在上面 → 铭文
words.example: The inscription on the tomb dates back to 300 BC.
words.example_zh: 墓碑上的铭文可以追溯到公元前300年。

words.meaning_en: lacking distinctive or interesting features or characteristics
words.derivation: non(不) + de(下) + script(写) → 写不下来特征的 → 不起眼的
words.example: The suspect drove away in a nondescript gray sedan.
words.example_zh: 嫌疑人开着一辆不起眼的灰色轿车离开了。

words.meaning_en: to forbid, especially by law; to denounce or condemn
words.derivation: pro(前) + scrib(写) → 在前面公开写出（罪行） → 禁止
words.example: The new regulations proscribe the use of certain chemicals in food production.
words.example_zh: 新规定禁止在食品生产中使用某些化学物质。

words.meaning_en: to restrict within limits; to draw a figure around another
words.derivation: circum(环绕) + scrib(画) → 在周围画线 → 限制
words.example: The Constitution circumscribes the powers of the federal government.
words.example_zh: 宪法限制了联邦政府的权力。

words.meaning_en: to enlist someone compulsorily for military service
words.derivation: con(一起) + script(写/登记) → 将名字一起登记在册 → 征召入伍
words.example: During the war, the government conscripted thousands of young men into the army.
words.example_zh: 战争期间，政府征召了数千名年轻人入伍。

words.meaning_en: to put thoughts, speech, or data into written form
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + scrib(写) → 跨越形式地写下 → 转录
words.example: The assistant was asked to transcribe the recorded interview into a written document.
words.example_zh: 助理被要求将录音采访转录成书面文件。

words.meaning_en: to attribute something to a cause or source
words.derivation: a(向) + scrib(写) → 向某处写上（原因） → 归因于
words.example: Historians ascribe the fall of the empire to a combination of internal and external factors.
words.example_zh: 历史学家将帝国的灭亡归因于内外因素的共同作用。

words.meaning_en: to carry or convey from one place to another
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + port(搬运) → 跨越搬运 → 运输
words.example: Trucks transport goods across the country.
words.example_zh: 卡车在全国各地运输货物。

words.meaning_en: to send goods or services to another country for sale
words.derivation: ex(向外) + port(运送) → 向外运送 → 出口
words.example: The country exports large quantities of oil.
words.example_zh: 该国出口大量石油。

words.meaning_en: to bring goods or services into a country from abroad
words.derivation: im(向内) + port(运送) → 向内运送 → 进口
words.example: Many countries import raw materials for manufacturing.
words.example_zh: 许多国家进口原材料用于制造业。

words.meaning_en: able to be easily carried or moved
words.derivation: port(携带) + able(能…的) → 能携带的 → 便携式的
words.example: She bought a portable computer for traveling.
words.example_zh: 她买了一台便携式电脑用于旅行。

words.meaning_en: to give a spoken or written account of something
words.derivation: re(回) + port(携带) → 带回消息 → 报告
words.example: The journalist will report on the latest developments.
words.example_zh: 记者将报道最新进展。

words.meaning_en: to expel a foreigner from a country
words.derivation: de(离开) + port(运送) → 运送离开 → 驱逐出境
words.example: The government decided to deport undocumented immigrants who had committed serious crimes.
words.example_zh: 政府决定驱逐犯下严重罪行的无证移民。

words.breakdown: de-(离开) + port-(运送) + -ation(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the act of expelling someone from a country
words.derivation: de(离开) + port(运送) + ation(名词后缀) → 运送离开的行为 → 驱逐
words.example: Deportation proceedings can take months or even years to complete.
words.example_zh: 驱逐程序可能需要数月甚至数年才能完成。

words.meaning_en: to organize and carry out; to lead or guide
words.derivation: con(共同) + duct(引导) → 一起引导 → 实施
words.example: The professor will conduct a series of experiments.
words.example_zh: 教授将进行一系列实验。

words.meaning_en: to bring something into use or operation for the first time
words.derivation: intro(向内) + duc(引导) → 引导进来 → 介绍
words.example: The company introduced a new product line last year.
words.example_zh: 该公司去年推出了新的产品线。

words.meaning_en: to make or manufacture from components or raw materials
words.derivation: pro(向前) + duc(引导) → 向前引出 → 生产
words.example: This factory produces electronic components.
words.example_zh: 这家工厂生产电子元件。

words.meaning_en: to make smaller or less in amount, degree, or size
words.derivation: re(回) + duc(引导) → 引导回去 → 减少
words.example: We need to reduce our carbon emissions.
words.example_zh: 我们需要减少碳排放。

words.meaning_en: to succeed in persuading or influencing someone to do something
words.derivation: in(向内) + duc(引导) → 引导进入某种状态 → 诱导
words.example: Stress can induce a variety of health problems.
words.example_zh: 压力可以引发各种健康问题。

words.meaning_en: to subtract or take away an amount from a total
words.derivation: de(离开) + duct(引导) → 引导离开 → 扣除
words.example: The company will deduct taxes directly from your monthly salary.
words.example_zh: 公司将直接从你的月薪中扣除税款。

words.meaning_en: to attract someone to a belief or into wrongdoing; to entice
words.derivation: se(离开) + duc(引导) → 引导离开正途 → 引诱
words.example: Advertisers use psychological techniques to seduce consumers into buying unnecessary products.
words.example_zh: 广告商利用心理技巧诱导消费者购买不必要的产品。

words.meaning_en: to take someone away illegally by force
words.derivation: ab(离开) + duct(引导) → 强行引走 → 绑架
words.example: The child was abducted from the playground but was safely returned within hours.
words.example_zh: 这个孩子在操场上被绑架，但在数小时内安全归来。

words.meaning_en: to look at something closely in order to assess its condition
words.derivation: in(向内) + spect(看) → 往里面看 → 检查
words.example: Officials will inspect the building for safety violations.
words.example_zh: 官员将检查建筑物是否存在安全违规。

words.breakdown: per-(通过) + spect-(看) + -ive(名词/形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a particular attitude toward or way of regarding something
words.derivation: per(通过) + spect(看) + ive(…的) → 通过某种方式去看 → 视角
words.example: Try to see the issue from a different perspective.
words.example_zh: 试着从不同的角度来看待这个问题。

words.meaning_en: a band of colors or a range of related values or qualities
words.derivation: spect(看) + rum(名词后缀) → 所看到的色带 → 光谱
words.example: The political spectrum ranges from left to right.
words.example_zh: 政治光谱从左到右分布。

words.meaning_en: an individual animal, plant, or piece of a mineral used as an example
words.derivation: spec(看) + imen(名词后缀) → 拿来看的东西 → 标本
words.example: The biologist collected a rare specimen in the forest.
words.example_zh: 生物学家在森林里采集了一个稀有标本。

words.meaning_en: a person who watches an event, especially a sports event
words.derivation: spect(看) + ator(…的人) → 看的人 → 观众
words.example: Thousands of spectators gathered to watch the game.
words.example_zh: 数千名观众聚集在一起观看比赛。

words.meaning_en: to change the form, character, or function of something
words.derivation: con(完全) + vert(转) → 完全转变 → 转换
words.example: The factory was converted into a museum.
words.example_zh: 这家工厂被改建成了博物馆。

words.meaning_en: showing a great deal of variety; very different
words.derivation: di(分开) + vers(转) → 向不同方向转 → 多样的
words.example: New York City has a diverse population.
words.example_zh: 纽约市拥有多元化的人口。

words.meaning_en: to move backward; to change to the opposite direction
words.derivation: re(回) + vers(转) → 转回去 → 反转
words.example: The court decided to reverse the previous ruling.
words.example_zh: 法院决定推翻先前的裁决。

words.breakdown: contra-(相反) + vers-(转) + -ial(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: giving rise or likely to give rise to public disagreement
words.derivation: contra(相反) + vers(转) + ial(…的) → 转向相反方向的 → 有争议的
words.example: The new policy is highly controversial among citizens.
words.example_zh: 新政策在市民中极具争议。

words.meaning_en: able to adapt or be adapted to many different functions
words.derivation: vers(转) + atile(能…的) → 能灵活转变的 → 多才多艺的
words.example: She is a versatile musician who plays several instruments.
words.example_zh: 她是一位多才多艺的音乐家，会演奏多种乐器。

words.meaning_en: to cause something to arise or come about
words.derivation: gen(产生) + er + ate(使) → 使之产生 → 生成
words.example: Wind turbines generate electricity from wind.
words.example_zh: 风力涡轮机利用风力发电。

words.meaning_en: all of the people born and living at about the same time
words.derivation: gen(出生) + er + ation(行为) → 一起出生的 → 一代人
words.example: The younger generation is more comfortable with technology.
words.example_zh: 年轻一代对科技更加适应。

words.meaning_en: a unit of heredity transferred from a parent to offspring
words.derivation: gen(产生) → 产生生命特征的单位 → 基因
words.example: Scientists have identified the gene responsible for this disease.
words.example_zh: 科学家已经确定了导致这种疾病的基因。

words.meaning_en: relating to genes or heredity
words.derivation: gen(起源) + etic(…的) → 与起源相关的 → 遗传的
words.example: Genetic research has advanced significantly in recent years.
words.example_zh: 近年来基因研究取得了重大进展。

words.breakdown: indi-(在…之内) + gen-(出生) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: originating or occurring naturally in a particular place; native
words.derivation: indi(在…之内) + gen(出生) + ous(…的) → 在当地出生的 → 本土的
words.example: These plants are indigenous to South America.
words.example_zh: 这些植物原产于南美洲。

words.breakdown: homo-(相同) + gen-(种类) + -eous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: of the same kind; uniform in composition
words.derivation: homo(相同) + gen(种类) + eous(…的) → 同种类的 → 同质的
words.example: The population in this region is relatively homogeneous in terms of cultural background.
words.example_zh: 这个地区的人口在文化背景方面相对同质。

words.breakdown: hetero-(不同) + gen-(种类) + -eous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: diverse in character or content; composed of different kinds
words.derivation: hetero(不同) + gen(种类) + eous(…的) → 不同种类的 → 异质的
words.example: Modern cities are increasingly heterogeneous, with residents from many different backgrounds.
words.example_zh: 现代城市日益多元化，居民来自许多不同的背景。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + gen-(产生/天性) + -ial(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: pleasant because of a personality or interests similar to one's own; agreeable
words.derivation: con(共同) + gen(天性) + ial(…的) → 天性相同的 → 意气相投的
words.example: She found the research team's atmosphere congenial and collaborative.
words.example_zh: 她觉得研究团队的氛围很融洽，也很协作。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + gen-(出生) + -ital(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: present from birth; inherent
words.derivation: con(一起) + gen(出生) + ital(…的) → 与生俱来的 → 先天的
words.example: Congenital heart defects are among the most common birth abnormalities.
words.example_zh: 先天性心脏缺陷是最常见的出生异常之一。

words.breakdown: in-(内在) + gen-(产生) + -ious(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: clever, original, and inventive
words.derivation: in(内在) + gen(产生) + ious(…的) → 内在产生才华的 → 巧妙的
words.example: The engineer devised an ingenious solution to the water purification problem.
words.example_zh: 工程师想出了一个巧妙的水净化问题解决方案。

words.breakdown: in-(内在) + gen-(产生/天性) + -uous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: innocent and unsuspecting; candid
words.derivation: in(内在) + gen(天性) + uous(…的) → 保持天性的 → 天真坦率的
words.example: Her ingenuous questions revealed a genuine curiosity about the world.
words.example_zh: 她天真的问题流露出对世界真诚的好奇心。

words.breakdown: de-(向下) + gen-(种/质) + -erate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to decline or deteriorate physically, mentally, or morally
words.derivation: de(向下) + gen(种/质) + erate → 品质向下走 → 退化/堕落
words.example: Without proper conservation, the coral reef will continue to degenerate.
words.example_zh: 没有适当的保护，珊瑚礁将继续退化。

words.meaning_en: to cause or give rise to a feeling, situation, or condition
words.derivation: en(使) + gen(产生) + der → 使产生 → 引起
words.example: The new policy engendered widespread debate among environmental scientists.
words.example_zh: 新政策在环境科学家中引起了广泛讨论。

words.breakdown: sym-(共同) + path-(感情) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: feelings of pity and sorrow for someone else's misfortune
words.derivation: sym(共同) + path(感情) + y(名词后缀) → 共同的感情 → 同情
words.example: She expressed sympathy for the victims of the disaster.
words.example_zh: 她对灾难受害者表示了同情。

words.breakdown: em-(在…之中) + path-(感情) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the ability to understand and share the feelings of another
words.derivation: em(进入) + path(感情) + y(名词后缀) → 进入他人的感情 → 共情
words.example: A good leader must have empathy for their team members.
words.example_zh: 一个好的领导者必须对团队成员有同理心。

words.breakdown: a-(没有) + path-(感情) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern
words.derivation: a(没有) + path(感情) + y(名词后缀) → 没有感情 → 冷漠
words.example: Voter apathy is a growing concern in many democracies.
words.example_zh: 选民的冷漠是许多民主国家日益关注的问题。

words.meaning_en: the science of the causes and effects of diseases
words.derivation: path(疾病) + logy(学科) → 研究疾病的学科 → 病理学
words.example: She studied pathology in medical school.
words.example_zh: 她在医学院学习了病理学。

words.meaning_en: arousing pity, especially through vulnerability or sadness
words.derivation: path(感情) + etic(…的) → 引发感情的 → 令人同情的
words.example: The stray dog presented a pathetic sight in the rain.
words.example_zh: 流浪狗在雨中的样子令人心生怜悯。

words.meaning_en: a system for transmitting voices over a distance using wire or radio
words.derivation: tele(远) + phon(声音) → 传到远处的声音 → 电话
words.example: Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone.
words.example_zh: 亚历山大·格雷厄姆·贝尔发明了电话。

words.meaning_en: an instrument for converting sound waves into electrical signals
words.derivation: micro(微小) + phon(声音) → 捕捉微小声音的装置 → 麦克风
words.example: The speaker adjusted the microphone before the presentation.
words.example_zh: 演讲者在演讲前调整了麦克风。

words.meaning_en: relating to the sounds of speech
words.derivation: phon(声音) + etic(…的) → 与声音有关的 → 语音的
words.example: The dictionary includes phonetic transcriptions of each word.
words.example_zh: 这本词典包含每个单词的音标。

words.breakdown: sym-(共同) + phon-(声音) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: an elaborate musical composition for a full orchestra
words.derivation: sym(共同) + phon(声音) + y(名词后缀) → 声音和谐共鸣 → 交响乐
words.example: Beethoven composed nine symphonies during his lifetime.
words.example_zh: 贝多芬一生创作了九部交响乐。

words.meaning_en: the quality of being pleasing to the ear
words.derivation: eu(好) + phon(声音) + y(名词后缀) → 好听的声音 → 悦耳
words.example: The poet chose words for their euphony as well as their meaning.
words.example_zh: 诗人选词既注重含义也注重声音的悦耳。

words.breakdown: caco-(坏) + phon-(声音) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: involving or producing a harsh, discordant mixture of sounds
words.derivation: caco(坏) + phon(声音) + ous(…的) → 坏的声音 → 刺耳的
words.example: The cacophonous noise from the construction site made it impossible to concentrate.
words.example_zh: 建筑工地刺耳的噪音让人无法集中注意力。

words.meaning_en: a picture made using a camera
words.derivation: photo(光) + graph(记录) → 用光记录 → 照片
words.example: The photograph captured a beautiful sunset over the ocean.
words.example_zh: 这张照片捕捉到了海面上美丽的日落。

words.meaning_en: an account of someone's life written by someone else
words.derivation: bio(生命) + graph(写) + y(名词后缀) → 写下一生 → 传记
words.example: She wrote a biography of the famous scientist.
words.example_zh: 她写了一部关于这位著名科学家的传记。

words.breakdown: geo-(地球) + graph-(写，描述) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the study of the physical features of the earth and its atmosphere
words.derivation: geo(地球) + graph(描述) + y(名词后缀) → 描述地球的学科 → 地理学
words.example: The geography of the region includes mountains and rivers.
words.example_zh: 该地区的地理特征包括山脉和河流。

words.meaning_en: a simplified drawing showing the structure of something
words.derivation: dia(通过) + gram(画) → 通过画来表达 → 图表
words.example: The professor drew a diagram to explain the process.
words.example_zh: 教授画了一张图表来解释这个过程。

words.meaning_en: a system for transmitting messages along a wire
words.derivation: tele(远) + graph(写) → 写到远方 → 电报
words.example: The telegraph revolutionized long-distance communication.
words.example_zh: 电报彻底改变了远距离通信方式。

words.meaning_en: the scientific study of living organisms
words.derivation: bio(生命) + logy(学科) → 研究生命的学科 → 生物学
words.example: She is majoring in biology at the university.
words.example_zh: 她在大学主修生物学。

words.meaning_en: the branch of biology dealing with organisms and their environment
words.derivation: eco(环境) + logy(学科) → 研究环境的学科 → 生态学
words.example: The professor teaches a course on marine ecology.
words.example_zh: 这位教授教授一门关于海洋生态学的课程。

words.meaning_en: the application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes
words.derivation: techno(技术) + logy(学科) → 关于技术的学科 → 科技
words.example: Technology has transformed the way we communicate.
words.example_zh: 科技改变了我们的交流方式。

words.meaning_en: a comparison between things that have similar features
words.derivation: ana(按照) + logy(推理) → 按照推理进行比较 → 类比
words.example: The teacher used an analogy to explain the complex concept.
words.example_zh: 老师用了一个类比来解释这个复杂的概念。

words.meaning_en: a conversation between two or more people
words.derivation: dia(之间) + log(语言) → 人与人之间的语言交流 → 对话
words.example: The novel contains realistic dialogue between its characters.
words.example_zh: 这部小说包含角色之间逼真的对话。

words.meaning_en: the variety of plant and animal life in the world or in a particular habitat
words.derivation: bio(生物) + diversity(多样性) → 生物的多样性 → 生物多样性
words.example: The rainforest is known for its incredible biodiversity.
words.example_zh: 热带雨林以其惊人的生物多样性而闻名。

words.meaning_en: the branch of science concerned with chemical processes in living organisms
words.derivation: bio(生命) + chemistry(化学) → 生命体中的化学 → 生物化学
words.example: She earned her degree in biochemistry.
words.example_zh: 她获得了生物化学学位。

words.breakdown: anti-(反对) + bio-(生命) + -tic(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a medicine that inhibits the growth of or destroys microorganisms
words.derivation: anti(反对) + bio(生命) + tic(…的) → 反对微生物生命的 → 抗生素
words.example: The doctor prescribed an antibiotic for the bacterial infection.
words.example_zh: 医生为细菌感染开了抗生素。

words.meaning_en: persisting for a long time or constantly recurring
words.derivation: chron(时间) + ic(…的) → 持续很长时间的 → 慢性的
words.example: He suffers from chronic back pain.
words.example_zh: 他患有慢性背痛。

words.meaning_en: a factual written account of important events in the order of their occurrence
words.derivation: chron(时间) + icle(名词后缀) → 按时间记录的文献 → 编年史
words.example: The book is a chronicle of the nation's history.
words.example_zh: 这本书是一部国家历史的编年史。

words.breakdown: chron-(时间) + -o- + log-(学科) + -ical(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: arranged in the order of time
words.derivation: chron(时间) + o + log(学科) + ical(…的) → 按时间逻辑排列的 → 按时间顺序的
words.example: Please list the events in chronological order.
words.example_zh: 请按时间顺序列出这些事件。

words.breakdown: syn-(共同) + chron-(时间) + -ize(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to cause to occur or operate at the same time or rate
words.derivation: syn(共同) + chron(时间) + ize(使) → 使时间一致 → 使同步
words.example: The dancers synchronized their movements perfectly.
words.example_zh: 舞者们完美地同步了他们的动作。

words.breakdown: ana-(向后) + chron-(时间) + -ism(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a thing belonging to a period other than that in which it exists
words.derivation: ana(向后) + chron(时间) + ism(现象) → 时间倒错的现象 → 时代错误
words.example: Using a typewriter in the digital age seems like an anachronism.
words.example_zh: 在数字时代使用打字机似乎是一种时代错误。

words.breakdown: syn-(同时) + chron-(时间) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: existing or occurring at the same time
words.derivation: syn(同时) + chron(时间) + ous(…的) → 同一时间的 → 同步的
words.example: The satellites maintain synchronous orbits around the Earth.
words.example_zh: 这些卫星保持着围绕地球的同步轨道。

words.meaning_en: to interrupt the normal course or unity of something
words.derivation: dis(分开) + rupt(断) → 打断使分开 → 扰乱
words.example: The protest disrupted traffic in the city center.
words.example_zh: 抗议活动扰乱了市中心的交通。

words.meaning_en: to burst out suddenly and violently
words.derivation: e(向外) + rupt(破) → 向外破裂 → 喷发
words.example: The volcano erupted without warning.
words.example_zh: 火山毫无预警地喷发了。

words.meaning_en: to stop the continuous progress of an activity or process
words.derivation: inter(在…之间) + rupt(断) → 在中间打断 → 中断
words.example: Please don't interrupt the speaker during the presentation.
words.example_zh: 请不要在演讲期间打断演讲者。

words.meaning_en: having or showing a willingness to act dishonestly for personal gain
words.derivation: cor(完全) + rupt(破) → 完全破坏(道德) → 腐败的
words.example: The corrupt officials were eventually brought to justice.
words.example_zh: 腐败官员最终被绳之以法。

words.meaning_en: sudden and unexpected
words.derivation: ab(离开) + rupt(断) → 突然断开 → 突然的
words.example: The meeting came to an abrupt end.
words.example_zh: 会议突然结束了。

words.meaning_en: able to be believed; convincing
words.derivation: cred(相信) + ible(能…的) → 能被相信的 → 可信的
words.example: The witness provided a credible account of the events.
words.example_zh: 证人对事件提供了可信的描述。

words.meaning_en: impossible to believe; extraordinary
words.derivation: in(不) + cred(相信) + ible(能…的) → 不能相信的 → 难以置信的
words.example: The speed of technological change is incredible.
words.example_zh: 技术变革的速度令人难以置信。

words.meaning_en: a qualification or achievement that proves capability
words.derivation: cred(信任) + ential(名词后缀) → 让人信任的凭证 → 资格证明
words.example: She presented her credentials to the hiring committee.
words.example_zh: 她向招聘委员会出示了自己的资格证明。

words.meaning_en: the ability to obtain goods before payment based on trust; recognition
words.derivation: cred(相信) + it → 基于相信给予的 → 信用
words.example: Students need 120 credits to graduate.
words.example_zh: 学生需要修满120个学分才能毕业。

words.meaning_en: to harm the good reputation of someone
words.derivation: dis(否定) + cred(相信) + it → 使人不再相信 → 使不可信
words.example: New evidence discredited the old theory.
words.example_zh: 新证据推翻了旧理论的可信度。

words.meaning_en: unwilling or unable to believe something; showing disbelief
words.derivation: in(不) + cred(相信) + ulous(倾向…的) → 不倾向于相信的 → 不轻信的
words.example: The audience was incredulous when the speaker claimed to have solved the decades-old problem.
words.example_zh: 当演讲者声称已经解决了这个几十年的难题时，听众表示难以置信。

words.meaning_en: a tendency to be too ready to believe that something is real or true
words.derivation: cred(相信) + ulity(性质) → 过于容易相信的性质 → 轻信
words.example: Scam artists often exploit the credulity of vulnerable populations.
words.example_zh: 诈骗分子经常利用弱势群体的轻信。

words.meaning_en: the body of words used in a particular language
words.derivation: voc(语言) + abul + ary(名词后缀) → 语言中可以叫出的词 → 词汇
words.example: Reading widely helps expand your vocabulary.
words.example_zh: 广泛阅读有助于扩大词汇量。

words.breakdown: ad-(朝向) + voc-(叫) + -ate(动词/名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to publicly recommend or support; a person who supports a cause
words.derivation: ad(朝向) + voc(叫) + ate(做) → 朝着某方向呼喊 → 提倡
words.example: She advocates for environmental protection.
words.example_zh: 她倡导环境保护。

words.meaning_en: to stimulate or give rise to a reaction or emotion
words.derivation: pro(向前) + vok(叫) → 向前叫喊挑衅 → 激怒
words.example: The controversial speech provoked widespread debate.
words.example_zh: 这篇有争议的演讲引发了广泛的辩论。

words.meaning_en: to bring or recall to the conscious mind
words.derivation: e(向外) + vok(叫) → 把记忆叫出来 → 唤起
words.example: The music evoked memories of her childhood.
words.example_zh: 这首音乐唤起了她童年的回忆。

words.meaning_en: relating to the human voice; expressing opinions freely
words.derivation: voc(声音) + al(…的) → 与声音有关的 → 直言不讳的
words.example: She has been vocal about the need for educational reform.
words.example_zh: 她一直在公开呼吁教育改革的必要性。

words.breakdown: a-(朝向) + vow-(誓言，源自voc-声音)
words.meaning_en: to assert or confess openly
words.derivation: a(朝向) + vow(誓言) → 朝着公众发誓 → 公开声明
words.example: The politician avowed his commitment to environmental protection.
words.example_zh: 这位政客公开声明他对环境保护的承诺。

words.meaning_en: vehement or clamorous in expressing views
words.derivation: voc(声音) + ifer(携带) + ous(…的) → 携带大声音的 → 大声叫嚷的
words.example: Vociferous protests erupted outside the government building.
words.example_zh: 政府大楼外爆发了激烈的抗议。

words.meaning_en: to call together for a meeting; to summon
words.derivation: con(一起) + vok(叫) → 叫到一起 → 召集
words.example: The president convoked an emergency session of Congress.
words.example_zh: 总统召集了国会紧急会议。

words.meaning_en: to officially cancel or annul
words.derivation: re(回) + vok(叫) → 叫回来 → 撤销
words.example: The court revoked his driver's license after the third offense.
words.example_zh: 法院在他第三次违规后吊销了他的驾照。

words.meaning_en: to cite as an authority; to call on for assistance
words.derivation: in(向内) + vok(叫) → 向内呼唤 → 援引
words.example: The lawyer invoked the Fifth Amendment to protect his client.
words.example_zh: 律师援引第五修正案来保护他的当事人。

words.meaning_en: to cause something to pass on from one place or person to another
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + mit(发送) → 跨越距离发送 → 传输
words.example: Radio waves transmit signals across long distances.
words.example_zh: 无线电波跨越远距离传输信号。

words.meaning_en: to present for consideration; to yield to authority
words.derivation: sub(在下方) + mit(送) → 从下方送上去 → 提交
words.example: Please submit your essay by Friday.
words.example_zh: 请在周五之前提交你的论文。

words.meaning_en: the production and discharge of something, especially gas or radiation
words.derivation: e(向外) + mis(送) + sion(行为) → 向外送出 → 排放
words.example: Carbon emissions contribute to global warming.
words.example_zh: 碳排放导致全球变暖。

words.meaning_en: to give authorization or consent to; an official document granting authorization
words.derivation: per(通过) + mit(送) → 让通过放行 → 允许
words.example: The city does not permit construction in this area.
words.example_zh: 该市不允许在这个区域进行施工。

words.meaning_en: an object forcibly propelled at a target
words.derivation: mis(发送) + sile(能…的) → 能被发射出去的 → 导弹
words.example: The military tested a new missile defense system.
words.example_zh: 军方测试了一种新型导弹防御系统。

words.meaning_en: a circumstance, fact, or influence that contributes to a result
words.derivation: fact(做) + or(…者) → 起作用的东西 → 因素
words.example: Cost is a major factor in the decision.
words.example_zh: 成本是决策中的一个重要因素。

words.breakdown: manu-(手) + fact-(制造) + -ure(名词/动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to make something on a large scale using machinery
words.derivation: manu(手) + fact(制造) + ure(行为) → 用手制造 → 生产
words.example: The company manufactures electronic devices.
words.example_zh: 这家公司制造电子设备。

words.meaning_en: a place provided for a particular purpose; ease of doing something
words.derivation: fac(做) + ility(名词后缀) → 方便做事的地方 → 设施
words.example: The university has excellent research facilities.
words.example_zh: 这所大学拥有出色的研究设施。

words.meaning_en: an object made by a human being, typically of historical interest
words.derivation: arti(技艺) + fact(制造) → 用技艺制造的东西 → 人工制品
words.example: The museum displays artifacts from ancient Egypt.
words.example_zh: 博物馆展出了来自古埃及的文物。

words.meaning_en: an inherent mental or physical power; the teaching staff of a university
words.derivation: fac(做) + ulty(名词后缀) → 有做事能力的人 → 全体教员
words.example: The faculty voted to change the curriculum.
words.example_zh: 全体教员投票决定修改课程。

words.breakdown: un-(不) + precedent-(先例) + -ed(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: never done or known before
words.derivation: un(不) + precedent(先例) + ed(…的) → 没有先例的 → 史无前例的
words.example: The country experienced unprecedented economic growth.
words.example_zh: 这个国家经历了史无前例的经济增长。

words.meaning_en: to estimate something to be smaller or less important than it actually is
words.derivation: under(低于) + estimate(估计) → 估计得过低 → 低估
words.example: Do not underestimate the difficulty of the exam.
words.example_zh: 不要低估这次考试的难度。

words.meaning_en: relating to or affecting all people in the world
words.derivation: uni(一) + vers(转) + al(…的) → 合为一体旋转的 → 普遍的
words.example: Access to clean water is a universal need.
words.example_zh: 获得清洁水源是全人类的普遍需求。

words.meaning_en: not likely to happen, be done, or be true
words.derivation: un(不) + likely(可能的) → 不可能的 → 不太可能的
words.example: It is unlikely that the project will finish on time.
words.example_zh: 这个项目不太可能按时完成。

words.meaning_en: to remove a cover from; to discover something hidden
words.derivation: un(去除) + cover(覆盖) → 去除覆盖 → 揭露
words.example: Archaeologists uncovered the remains of an ancient city.
words.example_zh: 考古学家发现了一座古城的遗迹。

words.meaning_en: to bring back to a former condition or position
words.derivation: re(回) + store(存放) → 放回原来的状态 → 恢复
words.example: The team worked to restore the damaged painting.
words.example_zh: 团队努力修复受损的画作。

words.meaning_en: to make previously unknown information known to others
words.derivation: re(回) + veal(面纱) → 揭开面纱 → 揭示
words.example: The study revealed a link between diet and disease.
words.example_zh: 这项研究揭示了饮食与疾病之间的关联。

words.meaning_en: to reexamine and make alterations to written or printed matter
words.derivation: re(再) + vis(看) → 再看一遍 → 修订
words.example: The author revised the manuscript several times.
words.example_zh: 作者多次修改了手稿。

words.meaning_en: capable of being renewed; not depleted when used
words.derivation: re(再) + new(新的) + able(能…的) → 能再次变新的 → 可再生的
words.example: Solar power is a renewable energy source.
words.example_zh: 太阳能是一种可再生能源。

words.meaning_en: to build or form something again after it has been damaged or destroyed
words.derivation: re(再) + construct(建造) → 再次建造 → 重建
words.example: Scientists are trying to reconstruct the ancient climate.
words.example_zh: 科学家正试图重建古代气候模型。

words.meaning_en: present as the strongest or main element
words.derivation: pre(在前) + dominant(支配的) → 在前面支配的 → 占主导地位的
words.example: English is the predominant language in international business.
words.example_zh: 英语是国际商务中的主导语言。

words.meaning_en: to maintain something in its original or existing state
words.derivation: pre(提前) + serve(保持) → 提前保持好 → 保存
words.example: We must preserve the natural environment for future generations.
words.example_zh: 我们必须为后代保护自然环境。

words.breakdown: pre-(在…之前) + limin-(门槛) + -ary(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: denoting an action or event preceding or done in preparation for something
words.derivation: pre(在…之前) + limin(门槛) + ary(…的) → 在门槛之前的 → 初步的
words.example: Preliminary results suggest the treatment is effective.
words.example_zh: 初步结果表明这种治疗方法是有效的。

words.breakdown: pre-(在…之前) + vi-(路) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: existing or occurring before in time or order
words.derivation: pre(在…之前) + vi(路) + ous(…的) → 走在前面的 → 先前的
words.example: She had no previous experience in this field.
words.example_zh: 她在这个领域没有以往的经验。

words.meaning_en: to come before something in time, order, or position
words.derivation: pre(在…之前) + ced(走) → 走在前面 → 先于
words.example: A brief introduction will precede the main lecture.
words.example_zh: 正式讲座之前会有一个简短的介绍。

words.breakdown: pre-(之前) + empt-(拿取) + -ive(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: serving or intended to preempt or forestall something
words.derivation: pre(之前) + empt(拿取) + ive(…的) → 提前拿走的 → 先发制人的
words.example: The country launched a preemptive military operation to prevent an anticipated invasion.
words.example_zh: 该国发动了一次先发制人的军事行动，以防止预期中的入侵。

words.breakdown: pre-(在…之前) + requis-(需要) + -ite(名词/形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a thing required as a prior condition for something else to happen
words.derivation: pre(之前) + requis(需要) + ite → 之前就需要的 → 先决条件
words.example: Calculus is a prerequisite for most advanced physics courses.
words.example_zh: 微积分是大多数高级物理课程的先决条件。

words.breakdown: pre-(预先) + dis-(分开) + posit-(放置) + -ion(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a tendency or inclination to a particular condition or quality
words.derivation: pre(预先) + dis(分开) + posit(放置) + ion(状态) → 预先被安排好的状态 → 倾向
words.example: Genetic predisposition plays a significant role in the development of certain diseases.
words.example_zh: 基因倾向在某些疾病的发展中起着重要作用。

words.breakdown: dis-(分开) + stingu-(刺，标记) + -ish(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to recognize or treat someone or something as different
words.derivation: dis(分开) + stingu(标记) + ish(做) → 用标记分开 → 区分
words.example: It is important to distinguish between fact and opinion.
words.example_zh: 区分事实和观点是很重要的。

words.meaning_en: to give shares of something; to spread over an area
words.derivation: dis(分开) + tribut(给予) → 分开给予 → 分配
words.example: The organization distributes food to those in need.
words.example_zh: 该组织向有需要的人分发食物。

words.meaning_en: to get rid of; to arrange in a particular position
words.derivation: dis(离开) + pos(放置) + e → 放到一边去 → 处置
words.example: You should dispose of hazardous waste properly.
words.example_zh: 你应该妥善处理危险废物。

words.meaning_en: to make visible by putting in a noticeable position
words.derivation: dis(展开) + play(折叠) → 展开折叠的东西 → 展示
words.example: The museum displays artifacts from the colonial period.
words.example_zh: 博物馆展出了殖民时期的文物。

words.meaning_en: to make or become less
words.derivation: di(分开) + min(小) + ish(使) → 使变得更小 → 减少
words.example: The supply of fresh water is diminishing rapidly.
words.example_zh: 淡水供应正在迅速减少。

words.meaning_en: to disperse or scatter; to squander or fritter away
words.derivation: dis(分散) + sip(扔) + ate → 四处抛散 → 消散/挥霍
words.example: The morning fog dissipated as the sun rose higher.
words.example_zh: 随着太阳升高，晨雾渐渐消散了。

words.meaning_en: to make a thorough or dramatic change in form or appearance
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + form(形式) → 跨越原有形式 → 转变
words.example: Technology has transformed modern education.
words.example_zh: 科技改变了现代教育。

words.meaning_en: the process of changing from one state or condition to another
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + it(走) + ion(行为) → 走过去到另一边 → 过渡
words.example: The transition from high school to college can be challenging.
words.example_zh: 从高中到大学的过渡可能充满挑战。

words.breakdown: trans-(通过) + par-(出现) + -ent(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: allowing light to pass through; easy to perceive or detect
words.derivation: trans(通过) + par(出现) + ent(…的) → 能透过去看到的 → 透明的
words.example: The government should be transparent about its spending.
words.example_zh: 政府应该对其支出保持透明。

words.meaning_en: to move or transfer something to another place or situation
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + plant(种植) → 跨越位置重新种植 → 移植
words.example: The surgeon performed a heart transplant.
words.example_zh: 外科医生进行了心脏移植手术。

words.meaning_en: to go beyond the range or limits of something
words.derivation: trans(超越) + scend(攀登) → 攀登并超越 → 超越
words.example: Great art can transcend cultural boundaries.
words.example_zh: 伟大的艺术可以超越文化的界限。

meaning_en: noun: act, state, or result of
meaning_zh: 名词后缀：表示行为、状态或结果

words.breakdown: civil-(公民) + -ize(使成为) + -ation(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the stage of human social and cultural development considered most advanced
words.derivation: civil(公民) + ize(使成为) + ation(状态) → 使成为公民社会的状态 → 文明
words.example: Ancient Greek civilization made lasting contributions to philosophy.
words.example_zh: 古希腊文明对哲学做出了持久的贡献。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + serv-(保持) + -ation(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the action of conserving something, especially the natural environment
words.derivation: con(共同) + serv(保持) + ation(行为) → 共同保持的行为 → 保护
words.example: Conservation of natural resources is essential for sustainability.
words.example_zh: 自然资源的保护对于可持续发展至关重要。

words.breakdown: migr-(迁移) + -ation(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the movement from one region or habitat to another
words.derivation: migr(迁移) + ation(行为) → 迁移的行为 → 迁移
words.example: Bird migration patterns are affected by climate change.
words.example_zh: 鸟类的迁徙模式受到气候变化的影响。

words.breakdown: ex-(向外) + pans-(展开) + -ion(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the action of becoming larger or more extensive
words.derivation: ex(向外) + pans(展开) + ion(行为) → 向外展开 → 扩张
words.example: The rapid expansion of the city caused environmental concerns.
words.example_zh: 城市的快速扩张引发了环境问题。

words.breakdown: e-(向外) + ros-(啃，侵蚀) + -ion(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the gradual destruction or diminution of something
words.derivation: e(向外) + ros(侵蚀) + ion(行为) → 向外啃蚀 → 侵蚀
words.example: Soil erosion is a major problem in deforested areas.
words.example_zh: 土壤侵蚀是森林被砍伐地区的一个主要问题。

meaning_en: adjective: full of, having the quality of
meaning_zh: 形容词后缀：充满…的，具有…性质的

words.breakdown: e-(超出) + norm-(标准) + -ous(形容词后缀：充满…的)
words.meaning_en: very large in size, quantity, or extent
words.derivation: e(超出) + norm(标准) + ous(…的) → 超出正常标准的 → 巨大的
words.example: The project requires an enormous amount of funding.
words.example_zh: 这个项目需要巨额资金。

words.breakdown: hazard(危险) + -ous(形容词后缀：充满…的)
words.meaning_en: risky; dangerous
words.derivation: hazard(危险) + ous(充满…的) → 充满危险的 → 有危险的
words.example: Hazardous chemicals must be stored carefully.
words.example_zh: 危险化学品必须小心存放。

words.breakdown: prosper(繁荣) + -ous(形容词后缀：充满…的)
words.meaning_en: successful in material terms; flourishing financially
words.derivation: prosper(繁荣) + ous(充满…的) → 充满繁荣的 → 繁荣的
words.example: The region became prosperous through trade.
words.example_zh: 该地区通过贸易变得繁荣昌盛。

words.breakdown: ambi-(两边) + gu-(驱动) + -ous(形容词后缀：具有…的)
words.meaning_en: open to more than one interpretation; unclear
words.derivation: ambi(两边) + gu(驱动) + ous(…的) → 被驱向两边的 → 模糊的
words.example: The instructions were ambiguous and confused the students.
words.example_zh: 说明含糊不清，让学生们感到困惑。

words.meaning_en: done openly and unashamedly; brazenly obvious
words.derivation: 源自拉丁语 blatire(胡说) → 大声而明目张胆的
words.example: The politician's blatant lies were exposed by investigative journalists.
words.example_zh: 这位政客的公然谎言被调查记者揭露了。

words.meaning_en: conspicuously or obviously offensive
words.derivation: flagr(燃烧) + ant(…的) → 像火一样明显的 → 公然的
words.example: The company's flagrant violation of safety regulations resulted in heavy fines.
words.example_zh: 该公司公然违反安全法规，导致了巨额罚款。

meaning_zh: 形容词后缀：能够…的，值得…的

words.meaning_en: able to be maintained at a certain rate or level
words.derivation: sustain(维持) + able(能…的) → 能够持续维持的 → 可持续的
words.example: We need to find sustainable sources of energy.
words.example_zh: 我们需要找到可持续的能源。

words.meaning_en: able to be reached or entered; easy to understand
words.derivation: access(接近) + ible(能…的) → 能够接近的 → 可接近的
words.example: The building is accessible to people with disabilities.
words.example_zh: 这座建筑对残障人士是无障碍的。

words.meaning_en: notably large in size, amount, or extent
words.derivation: consider(考虑) + able(值得…的) → 值得考虑的(规模) → 相当大的
words.example: The research required a considerable amount of time.
words.example_zh: 这项研究需要相当多的时间。

words.meaning_en: certain to happen; unavoidable
words.derivation: in(不) + evit(避免) + able(能…的) → 不能避免的 → 不可避免的
words.example: Change is inevitable in any growing organization.
words.example_zh: 在任何成长中的组织里，变化是不可避免的。

words.meaning_en: able to exist or occur together without conflict
words.derivation: com(共同) + pat(感受) + ible(能…的) → 能共同感受的 → 兼容的
words.example: The software is compatible with most operating systems.
words.example_zh: 这款软件与大多数操作系统兼容。

meaning_zh: 名词后缀：表示结果、行为或过程

words.breakdown: environ(围绕) + -ment(名词后缀：状态/结果)
words.meaning_en: the surroundings or conditions in which a person, animal, or plant lives
words.derivation: environ(围绕) + ment(状态) → 围绕在周围的状态 → 环境
words.example: Pollution is causing serious damage to the environment.
words.example_zh: 污染正在对环境造成严重损害。

words.meaning_en: a thing done successfully with effort, skill, or courage
words.derivation: achieve(实现) + ment(结果) → 实现的结果 → 成就
words.example: Landing on the moon was a remarkable achievement.
words.example_zh: 登上月球是一项非凡的成就。

words.breakdown: develop(发展) + -ment(名词后缀：过程/结果)
words.meaning_en: the process of developing or being developed
words.derivation: develop(发展) + ment(过程) → 发展的过程 → 发展
words.example: Economic development has improved living standards.
words.example_zh: 经济发展提高了生活水平。

words.breakdown: establish(建立) + -ment(名词后缀：结果/行为)
words.meaning_en: the action of establishing something; a business or organization
words.derivation: establish(建立) + ment(结果) → 建立的结果 → 机构
words.example: The establishment of the university dates back to 1850.
words.example_zh: 这所大学的建立可以追溯到1850年。

words.breakdown: assess(评估) + -ment(名词后缀：行为/结果)
words.meaning_en: the evaluation or estimation of the nature, quality, or ability of something
words.derivation: assess(评估) + ment(行为) → 评估的行为 → 评估
words.example: The teacher conducted a formal assessment of student progress.
words.example_zh: 老师对学生的进步进行了正式评估。

words.meaning_en: to undergo chemical breakdown; a state of agitation or excitement
words.derivation: fer(沸腾) + ment(过程) → 沸腾翻涌的过程 → 发酵/骚动
words.example: Social ferment in the 1960s led to significant political changes.
words.example_zh: 20世纪60年代的社会骚动导致了重大的政治变革。

meaning_en: verb: to make, to become, to treat as

words.meaning_en: to send a group of settlers to establish political control over a place
words.derivation: colon(殖民地) + ize(使成为) → 使成为殖民地 → 殖民
words.example: European powers colonized many parts of the world.
words.example_zh: 欧洲列强殖民了世界许多地方。

words.meaning_en: to concentrate on and become expert in a particular area
words.derivation: special(特别的) + ize(使成为) → 使自己变得特别 → 专攻
words.example: She decided to specialize in environmental law.
words.example_zh: 她决定专攻环境法。

words.meaning_en: to reduce something to the smallest possible amount or degree
words.derivation: minim(最小) + ize(使成为) → 使成为最小 → 最小化
words.example: We should minimize waste to protect the environment.
words.example_zh: 我们应该减少浪费来保护环境。

words.meaning_en: to give special importance or prominence to something
words.derivation: em(在…中) + phas(显示) + ize(使) → 使之显现出来 → 强调
words.example: The professor emphasized the importance of critical thinking.
words.example_zh: 教授强调了批判性思维的重要性。

words.meaning_en: to describe the distinctive nature or features of something
words.derivation: character(特征) + ize(使成为) → 使具有特征 → 以…为特征
words.example: Dry summers and mild winters characterize the Mediterranean climate.
words.example_zh: 干燥的夏季和温和的冬季是地中海气候的特征。

words.derivation: re(向后) + gress(走) → 向后走 → 倒退
words.meaning_en: to return to a former or less developed state
words.example: Without consistent practice, language skills tend to regress over time.
words.example_zh: 如果不坚持练习，语言技能会随着时间推移而退化。

words.derivation: in(进入) + gress(走) → 走进去 → 进入
words.meaning_en: the act of entering; a means of entering
words.example: The only ingress to the ancient tomb was through a narrow passageway carved into the rock.
words.example_zh: 进入这座古墓的唯一入口是凿在岩石中的一条狭窄通道。

words.derivation: trans(越过) + gress(走) → 走过界限 → 违反
words.meaning_en: to go beyond the bounds of a moral principle or law
words.example: Researchers who transgress ethical guidelines face severe professional consequences.
words.example_zh: 违反伦理准则的研究人员将面临严重的职业后果。

words.derivation: di(偏离) + gress(走) → 走偏了 → 离题
words.meaning_en: to leave the main subject temporarily in speech or writing
words.example: The lecturer tended to digress from the main topic, sharing personal anecdotes about fieldwork.
words.example_zh: 这位讲师经常偏离主题，分享关于实地考察的个人轶事。

words.derivation: ag(朝向) + gress(走) + ion(行为) → 向别人走去 → 侵犯
words.meaning_en: hostile or violent behavior or attitudes toward another
words.example: Studies show that aggression in primates often correlates with competition for limited resources.
words.example_zh: 研究表明，灵长类动物的攻击性通常与有限资源的竞争有关。

words.meaning_en: the action of going out of or leaving a place; an exit
words.derivation: e(出) + gress(行走) → 走出去 → 出口
words.example: Building codes require that all public venues provide adequate egress in case of emergency.
words.example_zh: 建筑法规要求所有公共场所在紧急情况下提供足够的出口。

words.derivation: ab(离开) + jure(发誓) → 发誓离开 → 发誓放弃
words.meaning_en: to solemnly renounce a belief, cause, or claim
words.example: The philosopher was forced to abjure his theories under pressure from religious authorities.
words.example_zh: 这位哲学家在宗教当局的压力下被迫公开放弃他的理论。

words.derivation: con(共同) + jure(发誓) → 一起发誓召唤 → 变魔术，想象
words.mnemonic: con(共同)+jure(发誓)→共同发誓召唤神灵→变魔术→凭空想象
words.meaning_en: to make something appear unexpectedly; to call upon a spirit by magic
words.example: The author conjures vivid images of prehistoric landscapes through detailed prose.
words.example_zh: 作者通过细致的文笔勾勒出栩栩如生的史前景观。

words.derivation: per(错误地) + jure(发誓) → 错误地发誓 → 作伪证
words.meaning_en: to willfully tell a lie when giving evidence in a court of law
words.example: Witnesses who perjure themselves risk imprisonment and permanent damage to their credibility.
words.example_zh: 作伪证的证人面临入狱和信誉永久受损的风险。

words.derivation: ad(朝向) + jure(发誓) → 对着某人发誓 → 恳求，嘱咐
words.meaning_en: to urge or request solemnly or earnestly
words.example: The judge adjured the witness to tell the truth about the events of that night.
words.example_zh: 法官恳请证人讲述那晚事件的真相。

words.derivation: abs(远离) + tain(握住) → 把自己拉住远离 → 戒除，弃权
words.meaning_en: to restrain oneself from doing something
words.example: Several committee members chose to abstain from the vote on the controversial proposal.
words.example_zh: 几位委员选择在这项有争议的提案上弃权。

words.derivation: de(加强) + tain(握住) → 牢牢握住不放 → 拘留
words.meaning_en: to keep someone from proceeding; to hold in custody
words.example: Border authorities may detain travelers whose documents appear to be fraudulent.
words.example_zh: 边境当局可以拘留证件疑似伪造的旅客。

words.derivation: re(回) + tain(握住) → 握回来 → 保留
words.meaning_en: to continue to have; to keep possession of
words.example: Desert plants have evolved specialized structures to retain moisture in arid conditions.
words.example_zh: 沙漠植物进化出了特殊结构，以在干旱条件下保持水分。

words.breakdown: at-(朝向) + tenu-(薄的) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.derivation: at(使) + tenu(薄) + ate(动作) → 使变薄 → 减弱，衰减
words.meaning_en: to reduce the force, effect, or value of something
words.example: The thick walls of the building attenuate outside noise, creating a quiet study environment.
words.example_zh: 建筑的厚墙减弱了外部噪音，创造了安静的学习环境。

words.derivation: per(贯穿) + tain(握住) → 始终握住关系 → 有关，适用
words.meaning_en: to be relevant or applicable to
words.example: The regulations pertain to all facilities that handle hazardous chemical waste.
words.example_zh: 这些规定适用于所有处理危险化学废物的设施。

words.derivation: af(朝向) + flu(流) + ence(状态) → 财富流向你 → 富裕
words.meaning_en: the state of having a great deal of money; wealth
words.example: The growing affluence of the middle class drove demand for consumer goods in the post-war era.
words.example_zh: 战后时代中产阶级日益增长的富裕推动了消费品的需求。

words.breakdown: super-(超过) + flu-(流) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.derivation: super(超过) + flu(流) + ous(…的) → 溢出来的 → 多余的
words.meaning_en: unnecessary, especially through being more than enough
words.example: The editor removed superfluous details that distracted from the paper's central argument.
words.example_zh: 编辑删除了分散论文核心论点注意力的多余细节。

words.derivation: fluct(流动) + uate(使) → 像水流般起伏 → 波动
words.meaning_en: to rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
words.example: Ocean temperatures fluctuate seasonally, affecting the distribution of marine species.
words.example_zh: 海洋温度随季节波动，影响着海洋生物的分布。

words.derivation: in(进入) + flux(流) → 流入 → 涌入
words.meaning_en: an arrival or entry of large numbers of people or things
words.example: The influx of immigrants in the early 1900s transformed the demographic landscape of American cities.
words.example_zh: 二十世纪初移民的大量涌入改变了美国城市的人口结构。

words.derivation: ef(向外) + fluv(流) + ia(物) → 流出来的东西 → 废气，臭气
words.meaning_en: an unpleasant or harmful outflow of gas or waste
words.example: The effluvia from the industrial plant contaminated the surrounding soil and groundwater.
words.example_zh: 工厂排出的废气污染了周围的土壤和地下水。

words.derivation: hier(神圣的) + archy(统治) → 神圣的统治秩序 → 等级制度
words.meaning_en: a system in which members are ranked according to status or authority
words.example: In many traditional societies, a strict social hierarchy determined access to resources and power.
words.example_zh: 在许多传统社会中，严格的社会等级制度决定了资源和权力的获取。

words.derivation: an(无) + archy(统治) → 没有统治 → 无政府状态
words.meaning_en: a state of disorder due to absence of authority
words.example: The collapse of the central government plunged the region into a period of anarchy.
words.example_zh: 中央政府的崩溃使该地区陷入了一段无政府状态。

words.derivation: mon(单独) + arch(统治者) → 独自统治的人 → 君主
words.meaning_en: a sovereign head of state, especially a king or queen
words.example: The monarch wielded absolute power before the constitutional reforms of the 18th century.
words.example_zh: 在十八世纪宪法改革之前，君主拥有绝对权力。

words.derivation: olig(少数) + archy(统治) → 少数人统治 → 寡头政治
words.meaning_en: a form of government in which power rests with a small number of people
words.example: Critics argue that the concentration of wealth has transformed the democracy into an oligarchy.
words.example_zh: 批评人士认为财富集中已将民主制度变成了寡头政治。

words.meaning_en: a system of government by one person with absolute power
words.derivation: auto(自己) + cracy(统治) → 一个人统治 → 独裁
words.example: The transition from autocracy to democracy was gradual and difficult.
words.example_zh: 从专制到民主的转变是渐进而艰难的。

words.meaning_en: a system of government with many complicated rules and processes
words.derivation: bureau(办公室) + cracy(统治) → 办公室统治 → 官僚体制
words.example: Excessive bureaucracy can slow down the implementation of new policies.
words.example_zh: 过度的官僚主义会拖慢新政策的实施。

words.meaning_en: the highest class in certain societies; government by the best-born
words.derivation: aristo(最优秀的) + cracy(统治) → 最优秀者统治 → 贵族政治
words.example: The French Revolution challenged the power of the aristocracy.
words.example_zh: 法国大革命挑战了贵族阶级的权力。

words.meaning_en: a system of government by the whole population, typically through elected representatives
words.derivation: demo(人民) + cracy(统治) → 人民统治 → 民主
words.example: Democracy requires the active participation of informed citizens.
words.example_zh: 民主需要知情公民的积极参与。

words.derivation: as(朝向) + sent(感觉) → 对某事有同感 → 赞同
words.meaning_en: to express approval or agreement
words.example: The board members gave their assent to the proposed budget for the fiscal year.
words.example_zh: 董事会成员同意了本财政年度的预算方案。

words.derivation: con(共同) + sent(感觉) → 有共同感觉 → 同意，许可
words.meaning_en: permission for something to happen or agreement to do something
words.example: Informed consent is a fundamental requirement in any ethical research involving human subjects.
words.example_zh: 知情同意是任何涉及人类受试者的伦理研究的基本要求。

words.derivation: dis(分离) + sent(感觉) → 感觉不同 → 异议
words.meaning_en: the expression of opinions at variance with those commonly held
words.example: Political dissent was harshly suppressed during the authoritarian regime.
words.example_zh: 在专制政权统治期间，政治异议遭到了严厉镇压。

words.derivation: sent(感觉) + iment(状态) → 内心感觉 → 情感，观点
words.meaning_en: a view or attitude toward a situation; a feeling or emotion
words.example: Public sentiment toward environmental conservation has shifted dramatically in recent decades.
words.example_zh: 近几十年来公众对环境保护的情绪发生了巨大变化。

words.derivation: sens(感觉) + ory(与…有关的) → 与感觉有关的 → 感觉的
words.meaning_en: relating to sensation or the physical senses
words.example: Sensory deprivation experiments have revealed much about the brain's need for external stimulation.
words.example_zh: 感觉剥夺实验揭示了大脑对外部刺激需求的许多信息。

words.derivation: amor(爱) + ous(充满…的) → 充满爱的 → 多情的
words.meaning_en: showing or feeling sexual desire and love
words.example: Renaissance poets often explored amorous themes in their sonnets and verse.
words.example_zh: 文艺复兴时期的诗人经常在十四行诗和诗歌中探讨爱情主题。

words.derivation: ami(爱) + able(能够…的) → 能让人喜爱的 → 和蔼可亲的
words.meaning_en: having a friendly and pleasant manner
words.example: The researcher's amiable demeanor put nervous interview subjects at ease.
words.example_zh: 研究人员和蔼可亲的态度使紧张的受访者放松了下来。

words.derivation: amic(朋友) + able(能够…的) → 能友好相处的 → 友好的
words.meaning_en: characterized by friendliness and absence of discord
words.example: After years of conflict, the two nations finally reached an amicable settlement.
words.example_zh: 经过多年冲突，两国终于达成了友好解决方案。

words.derivation: en(使) + amor(爱) → 使产生爱 → 使迷恋
words.meaning_en: to be filled with a feeling of love for; to charm or captivate
words.example: Audiences were immediately enamored of the new archaeological discovery from the Bronze Age.
words.example_zh: 观众立即被这一青铜时代的新考古发现所吸引。

words.derivation: en(否定) + am(爱) + ity(状态) → 没有爱的状态 → 敌意
words.mnemonic: enemy(敌人)的名词形式→敌意；与amor(爱)相反，记住en-在此表否定
words.meaning_en: the state of being actively opposed or hostile to someone
words.example: Deep enmity between the rival factions made diplomatic negotiations nearly impossible.
words.example_zh: 敌对派系之间根深蒂固的仇恨使外交谈判几乎不可能进行。

words.breakdown: re-(再次) + nov-(新的) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.derivation: re(再次) + nov(新) + ate(使) → 使再次变新 → 翻新
words.meaning_en: to restore to a good state of repair; to make new again
words.example: The city invested millions to renovate historic buildings in the downtown district.
words.example_zh: 该市投资数百万翻新市中心区的历史建筑。

words.breakdown: in-(进入) + nov-(新的) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.derivation: in(进入) + nov(新) + ate(使) → 引入新事物 → 创新
words.meaning_en: to make changes in something established by introducing new methods or ideas
words.example: Companies that fail to innovate often lose their competitive advantage in rapidly evolving markets.
words.example_zh: 在快速发展的市场中，无法创新的公司往往会失去竞争优势。

words.derivation: nov(新) + elty(性质) → 新的性质 → 新奇
words.meaning_en: the quality of being new, original, or unusual
words.example: The novelty of the discovery attracted widespread attention from the scientific community.
words.example_zh: 这一发现的新颖性引起了科学界的广泛关注。

words.derivation: nov(新) + ice(人) → 新来的人 → 新手
words.meaning_en: a person new to or inexperienced in a field or situation
words.example: Even a novice researcher can appreciate the elegance of the experimental design.
words.example_zh: 即使是初学者也能欣赏到这一实验设计的精妙之处。

words.derivation: an(使) + nihil(无) + ate(动作) → 使变成无 → 消灭
words.meaning_en: to destroy utterly; to obliterate
words.example: The asteroid impact annihilated the majority of dinosaur species 66 million years ago.
words.example_zh: 6600万年前的小行星撞击消灭了大多数恐龙物种。

words.derivation: null(无) + ify(使) → 使变为无 → 使无效
words.meaning_en: to make legally null and void; to invalidate
words.example: The court's ruling effectively nullified the previous legislation on land use.
words.example_zh: 法院的裁决实际上废除了之前的土地使用法规。

words.derivation: an(使) + nul(无) → 使变成无 → 废除，取消
words.meaning_en: to declare invalid; to abolish
words.example: The government moved to annul contracts that had been awarded through corrupt practices.
words.example_zh: 政府采取行动废除了那些通过腐败行为授予的合同。

words.breakdown: an-(朝向) + not-(标记) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.derivation: an(加强) + not(标记) + ate(做) → 加上标记 → 注释
words.meaning_en: to add notes of explanation or comment to a text
words.example: Students are encouraged to annotate primary sources when preparing for the research seminar.
words.example_zh: 学生在准备研究研讨会时被鼓励对原始资料进行注释。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + not-(标记) + -ation(名词后缀)
words.derivation: con(共同) + not(标记) + ation(行为) → 共同附带的标记 → 言外之意
words.meaning_en: an idea or feeling that a word invokes in addition to its literal meaning
words.example: The word 'home' carries connotations of warmth and security beyond its literal definition.
words.example_zh: “家”这个词除了字面意义之外，还带有温暖和安全的言外之意。

words.derivation: not(知道) + orious(…的) → 广为人知的（贬义）→ 臭名昭著的
words.meaning_en: famous for something bad; widely and unfavorably known
words.meaning_zh: 臭名昭著的，声名狼藉的
words.example: The region is notorious for its unpredictable weather patterns that disrupt agricultural cycles.
words.example_zh: 该地区因其扰乱农业周期的不可预测天气模式而臭名昭著。

words.derivation: not(标记) + ation(行为) → 标记的方式 → 记号，标记法
words.meaning_en: a system of symbols used to represent numbers, amounts, or elements
words.example: Scientific notation allows researchers to express extremely large or small numbers efficiently.
words.example_zh: 科学记数法使研究人员能够高效地表达极大或极小的数字。

words.breakdown: de-(向下) + not-(标记) + -ation(名词后缀)
words.derivation: de(完全) + not(标记) + ation(行为) → 完全标出的意思 → 字面意义
words.meaning_en: the literal or primary meaning of a word
words.example: In linguistics, the denotation of a word is distinguished from its emotional connotation.
words.example_zh: 在语言学中，一个词的字面意义与其情感含义是有区别的。

words.derivation: aqu(水) + atic(与…有关的) → 与水有关的 → 水生的
words.meaning_en: relating to or living in water
words.example: Aquatic ecosystems are particularly vulnerable to the effects of industrial pollution.
words.example_zh: 水生生态系统特别容易受到工业污染的影响。

words.derivation: aqu(水) + ifer(承载) → 承载水的地层 → 含水层
words.meaning_en: an underground layer of rock that holds groundwater
words.example: Over-extraction from the aquifer has caused water tables to drop significantly in the region.
words.example_zh: 对含水层的过度抽取已导致该地区地下水位大幅下降。

words.derivation: aqu(水) + duct(引导) → 引导水的建筑 → 水渠
words.meaning_en: an artificial channel for conveying water
words.example: Roman aqueducts represent remarkable feats of ancient engineering that supplied cities with fresh water.
words.example_zh: 罗马水渠是古代工程的杰出成就，为城市供应了淡水。

words.derivation: ard(燃烧) + ent(…的) → 像火一样燃烧的 → 热情的
words.meaning_en: enthusiastic or passionate
words.example: The ardent supporters of conservation lobbied tirelessly for the protection of endangered habitats.
words.example_zh: 热情的环保支持者不知疲倦地为保护濒危栖息地而游说。

words.derivation: ard(燃烧) + uous(…的) → 像火烧一样煎熬的 → 艰苦的
words.meaning_en: involving or requiring strenuous effort; difficult and tiring
words.example: The arduous journey across the mountain range tested the limits of the expedition team.
words.example_zh: 穿越山脉的艰苦旅程考验了探险队的极限。

words.derivation: ars(燃烧) + on(行为) → 故意纵火的行为 → 纵火罪
words.meaning_en: the criminal act of deliberately setting fire to property
words.example: Investigators determined that arson was the cause of the warehouse fire that destroyed rare specimens.
words.example_zh: 调查人员确定纵火是导致仓库起火、摧毁珍贵标本的原因。

words.derivation: ard(燃烧) + or(状态) → 内心燃烧的状态 → 热忱
words.meaning_en: enthusiasm or passion
words.example: The young scientist pursued her research with an ardor that inspired her colleagues.
words.example_zh: 这位年轻科学家以激励同事们的热忱投入研究工作。

words.breakdown: arr-(变体自err-漫游) + -ant(形容词后缀)
words.derivation: err(漫游) → 四处游荡的 → 彻头彻尾的（贬义加强）
words.mnemonic: arrant是errant的变体，从「到处游荡」引申为「臭名远扬的、彻底的」
words.meaning_en: complete; utter (used to emphasize something bad)
words.meaning_zh: 彻头彻尾的，十足的（贬义）
words.example: The claim that climate change is a hoax is arrant nonsense contradicted by overwhelming evidence.
words.example_zh: 声称气候变化是骗局完全是十足的胡说八道，已被大量证据所反驳。

words.derivation: err(漫游) + ant(…的) → 到处漫游的 → 漫游的，犯错的
words.meaning_en: erring or straying from the proper course; traveling in search of adventure
words.meaning_zh: 漫游的，偏离正道的，犯错误的
words.example: An errant weather pattern brought unexpected rainfall to the typically arid region.
words.example_zh: 一个异常的天气模式给这个通常干旱的地区带来了意外的降雨。

words.derivation: err(漫游) + atic(…的) → 到处游走不定的 → 不稳定的
words.meaning_en: not even or regular in pattern; unpredictable
words.example: The erratic migration patterns of the species have puzzled ornithologists for decades.
words.example_zh: 这一物种反复无常的迁徙模式困扰了鸟类学家数十年。

words.breakdown: ab-(偏离) + err-(漫游) + -ant(形容词后缀)
words.derivation: ab(偏离) + err(漫游) + ant(…的) → 偏离正常的 → 异常的
words.meaning_en: departing from an accepted standard; abnormal
words.example: Aberrant cell growth is one of the earliest indicators of potential tumor development.
words.example_zh: 异常的细胞生长是潜在肿瘤发展的最早指标之一。

words.derivation: err(错误) + oneous(…的) → 包含错误的 → 错误的
words.meaning_en: wrong; incorrect
words.example: The erroneous assumption that the Earth was flat persisted for centuries before being disproved.
words.example_zh: 地球是平的这一错误假设持续了几个世纪才被推翻。

words.breakdown: as-(朝向) + sid-(坐) + -uous(形容词后缀)
words.derivation: as(一直) + sid(坐) + uous(…的) → 一直坐着做事的 → 勤勉的
words.meaning_en: showing great care and perseverance
words.example: The assiduous researcher spent years compiling data before publishing her groundbreaking findings.
words.example_zh: 这位勤勉的研究人员花了数年时间收集数据，才发表了她的突破性发现。

words.breakdown: in-(在…中) + sid-(坐) + -ious(形容词后缀)
words.derivation: in(在…中) + sid(坐) + ious(…的) → 坐在暗处伏击的 → 阴险的
words.meaning_en: proceeding in a gradual, subtle way with harmful effects
words.example: The insidious effects of lead contamination may not become apparent for years after exposure.
words.example_zh: 铅污染的潜伏性影响可能在接触后多年才会显现。

words.derivation: pre(在前) + sid(坐) → 坐在前面 → 主持
words.meaning_en: to be in the position of authority in a meeting or gathering
words.example: The dean was invited to preside over the academic conference on climate policy.
words.example_zh: 院长被邀请主持这场关于气候政策的学术会议。

words.derivation: sub(向下) + sid(坐) → 坐下去 → 下沉，减退
words.meaning_en: to become less intense, violent, or severe
words.example: After the volcanic eruption subsided, scientists began surveying the altered landscape.
words.example_zh: 火山喷发平息后，科学家们开始勘察被改变的地貌。

words.derivation: sed(坐) + entary(与…有关的) → 与久坐有关的 → 久坐不动的
words.meaning_en: tending to spend much time seated; somewhat inactive
words.example: A sedentary lifestyle has been linked to increased risks of cardiovascular disease and obesity.
words.example_zh: 久坐不动的生活方式与心血管疾病和肥胖风险的增加有关。

words.breakdown: sed-(坐，安静) + -ate(动词/形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: calm, dignified, and unhurried; to administer a sedative to
words.meaning_zh: 沉着的，镇静的；给…用镇静剂
words.derivation: sed(坐) + ate → 安静地坐着的 → 沉着的
words.example: The veterinarian had to sedate the animal before performing the surgery.
words.example_zh: 兽医在做手术前必须给动物注射镇静剂。

words.meaning_en: matter that settles to the bottom of a liquid
words.derivation: sed(坐，沉下来) + iment → 沉下来的东西 → 沉积物
words.example: Layers of sediment at the bottom of the lake provide clues about past climate conditions.
words.example_zh: 湖底的沉积层为了解过去的气候条件提供了线索。

words.breakdown: as-(朝向) + simil-(相似) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.derivation: as(朝向) + simil(相似) + ate(使) → 使变得相似 → 同化，吸收
words.meaning_en: to absorb and integrate; to cause to resemble
words.example: Immigrant communities often struggle to assimilate into the dominant culture while preserving their own traditions.
words.example_zh: 移民社区在融入主流文化的同时保持自身传统往往面临困难。

words.derivation: simul(同时) + taneous(…的) → 在同一时间的 → 同时的
words.meaning_en: occurring, operating, or done at the same time
words.example: The simultaneous eruption of multiple volcanoes along the fault line reshaped the entire region.
words.example_zh: 沿断层线多座火山同时喷发重塑了整个地区的面貌。

words.breakdown: dis-(否定) + simul-(相似) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.derivation: dis(否定) + simul(相似) + ate(使) → 使看起来不像真实的 → 掩饰
words.meaning_en: to conceal or disguise one's true feelings or intentions
words.meaning_zh: 掩饰，隐藏（真实感情或意图）
words.example: Political leaders often dissimulate their true motives behind rhetoric about public welfare.
words.example_zh: 政治领袖常常用关于公共福利的言辞来掩饰其真实动机。

words.derivation: dis(否定) + semble(相似) → 使看起来不像 → 掩饰，伪装
words.mnemonic: 注意与disassemble(拆卸)区分：dissemble是掩饰真面目，dis+semble(像)→不像真实的样子
words.meaning_en: to hide one's true motives or feelings behind a false appearance
words.example: The politician tried to dissemble his involvement in the scandal, but evidence proved otherwise.
words.example_zh: 那位政客试图掩饰自己与丑闻的牵涉，但证据证明并非如此。

words.meaning_en: to imitate the appearance or character of
words.derivation: simul(相似) + ate(动词后缀) → 使相似 → 模拟
words.example: Computer programs can simulate complex weather patterns to predict future climate conditions.
words.example_zh: 计算机程序可以模拟复杂的天气模式来预测未来的气候条件。

words.meaning_en: to pretend to be affected by a feeling or condition
words.derivation: feign(假装/虚构) → 假装
words.example: The student feigned illness to avoid taking the difficult exam.
words.example_zh: 这个学生假装生病以逃避困难的考试。

words.meaning_en: a large enclosure for keeping birds
words.derivation: avi(鸟) + ary(场所) → 鸟的场所 → 鸟舍
words.example: The zoo's aviary houses over fifty species of tropical birds.
words.example_zh: 动物园的鸟舍里饲养着五十多种热带鸟类。

words.meaning_en: the flying or operating of aircraft
words.derivation: avi(鸟) + ation(行为) → 像鸟一样飞的行为 → 航空
words.example: The aviation industry has grown rapidly in the last decade.
words.example_zh: 航空业在过去十年中发展迅速。

words.meaning_en: conducive to success; favorable
words.derivation: au(鸟) + spic(看) + ious(…的) → 观鸟占卜为吉 → 吉利的
words.example: The sunny weather was an auspicious sign for the outdoor ceremony.
words.example_zh: 晴朗的天气对户外典礼来说是个好兆头。

words.meaning_en: an improvised barrier erected to block a route
words.derivation: barr(障碍) + icade → 用来阻挡的东西 → 路障
words.example: Protesters set up barricades across the main road.
words.example_zh: 抗议者在主干道上设置了路障。

words.meaning_en: a fence or obstacle that prevents movement or access
words.derivation: barr(障碍) + ier → 阻挡物 → 障碍
words.example: Language can be a major barrier to communication.
words.example_zh: 语言可能是沟通的一大障碍。

words.meaning_en: an official ban on trade or other commercial activity
words.derivation: em(进入) + bar(阻拦) + go → 进入阻拦状态 → 禁运
words.example: The government imposed an embargo on oil exports.
words.example_zh: 政府对石油出口实施了禁运。

words.meaning_en: too poor to produce much vegetation; not productive
words.derivation: barr(阻碍) + en → 生长受阻的 → 贫瘠的
words.example: The barren landscape stretched for miles without a single tree.
words.example_zh: 贫瘠的大地绵延数英里，看不到一棵树。

words.meaning_en: the killing of a large number of people
words.derivation: carn(肉) + age(行为) → 变成肉的行为 → 大屠杀
words.example: The battlefield was a scene of terrible carnage.
words.example_zh: 战场上一片惨烈的杀戮景象。

words.meaning_en: relating to physical, especially sexual, needs and activities
words.derivation: carn(肉) + al(…的) → 肉体的
words.example: The novel explored both spiritual and carnal desires.
words.example_zh: 这部小说探讨了精神和肉体的欲望。

words.breakdown: re-(再) + in-(进入) + carn-(肉) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to be born again in another body
words.derivation: re(再) + in(进入) + carn(肉) + ate → 再次进入肉体 → 转世
words.example: Some religions believe that the soul is reincarnated after death.
words.example_zh: 一些宗教相信灵魂在死后会转世。

words.meaning_en: the formal giving up of rights, property, or territory
words.derivation: cess(让步) + ion(行为) → 让步的行为 → 割让
words.example: The treaty required the cession of territory to the victorious nation.
words.example_zh: 该条约要求向战胜国割让领土。

words.meaning_en: to admit that something is true after first denying it; to yield
words.derivation: con(完全) + cede(让步) → 完全让步 → 承认
words.example: The candidate conceded the election after the final votes were counted.
words.example_zh: 最终计票后，候选人承认了选举失败。

words.meaning_en: to withdraw formally from membership in an organization or alliance
words.derivation: se(分开) + cede(走) → 分开走 → 退出
words.example: Several southern states attempted to secede from the Union.
words.example_zh: 几个南方州试图脱离联邦。

words.meaning_en: to intervene on behalf of another
words.derivation: inter(之间) + cede(走) → 走到中间 → 调解
words.example: The teacher interceded when the argument between students became heated.
words.example_zh: 当学生之间的争论变得激烈时，老师出面调解。

words.meaning_en: to agree to a demand, request, or treaty
words.derivation: ac(朝向) + cede(让步) → 朝着让步方向走 → 同意
words.example: The government acceded to the protesters' demands for reform.
words.example_zh: 政府同意了抗议者的改革要求。

words.meaning_en: severely critical of others
words.derivation: cens(判断) + orious(…的) → 爱判断别人的 → 好批评的
words.example: Her censorious tone made everyone uncomfortable during the meeting.
words.example_zh: 她挑剔的语气让会议上的每个人都不自在。

words.meaning_en: to express severe disapproval of someone, typically in a formal statement
words.derivation: cens(判断) + ure(行为) → 判断后的行为 → 谴责
words.example: The senator faced censure from colleagues for his unethical behavior.
words.example_zh: 这位参议员因不道德行为遭到同僚的谴责。

words.meaning_en: an official count or survey of a population
words.derivation: cens(评估) + us → 对人口的评估 → 人口普查
words.example: The national census is conducted every ten years.
words.example_zh: 全国人口普查每十年进行一次。

words.meaning_en: the use of many words where fewer would do; evasive speech
words.derivation: circum(周围) + locut(说) + ion(行为) → 绕着圈子说话 → 迂回说法
words.example: Politicians are often criticized for their circumlocution when answering direct questions.
words.example_zh: 政客们在回答直接问题时经常因为绕圈子而受到批评。

words.meaning_en: fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing
words.derivation: e(出) + loqu(说) + ent(…的) → 说出来有力量的 → 雄辩的
words.example: The lawyer delivered an eloquent closing argument that moved the jury.
words.example_zh: 律师发表了一段雄辩的结案陈词，打动了陪审团。

words.meaning_en: tending to talk a great deal; talkative
words.derivation: loqu(说) + acious(倾向于) → 倾向于说话的 → 话多的
words.example: My loquacious neighbor can talk for hours without stopping.
words.example_zh: 我那位话多的邻居能不停地说上几个小时。

words.meaning_en: a formal conversation or dialogue
words.derivation: col(共同) + loqu(说) + y → 一起说话 → 正式会谈
words.example: The professors engaged in a lengthy colloquy about the research findings.
words.example_zh: 教授们就研究发现进行了长时间的学术对话。

words.meaning_en: an act of speaking one's thoughts aloud when alone, especially in a play
words.derivation: soli(独自) + loqu(说) + y → 独自说话 → 独白
words.example: Hamlet's famous soliloquy begins with 'To be, or not to be.'
words.example_zh: 哈姆雷特著名的独白以「生存还是毁灭」开始。

words.meaning_en: to tell someone about a secret or private matter in trust
words.derivation: con(完全) + fid(信任) + e → 完全信任 → 吐露秘密
words.example: She confided her fears to her closest friend.
words.example_zh: 她向最亲密的朋友吐露了自己的恐惧。

words.meaning_en: faithfulness to a person, cause, or belief
words.derivation: fid(信任) + elity(性质) → 信任的性质 → 忠诚
words.example: The dog showed remarkable fidelity to its owner throughout the years.
words.example_zh: 这条狗多年来对主人表现出了非凡的忠诚。

words.breakdown: dif-(否定) + fid-(信任) + -ent(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: modest or shy because of a lack of self-confidence
words.derivation: dif(不) + fid(信任) + ent(…的) → 不信任自己的 → 缺乏自信的
words.example: The diffident student rarely spoke up in class discussions.
words.example_zh: 这个缺乏自信的学生在课堂讨论中很少发言。

words.breakdown: per-(通过/破坏) + fid-(信任) + -ious(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: deceitful and untrustworthy
words.derivation: per(破坏) + fid(信任) + ious(…的) → 破坏信任的 → 背信弃义的
words.example: The perfidious ally betrayed their trust at the worst possible moment.
words.example_zh: 那个背信弃义的盟友在最糟糕的时刻背叛了他们的信任。

words.meaning_en: to make narrower by squeezing; to tighten
words.derivation: con(一起) + strict(收紧) → 紧紧收缩到一起 → 压缩
words.example: Cold temperatures cause blood vessels to constrict.
words.example_zh: 低温会导致血管收缩。

words.meaning_en: to put a limit on; to keep under control
words.derivation: re(回) + strict(收紧) → 收回来束缚住 → 限制
words.example: The new policy restricts the number of visitors allowed in the park.
words.example_zh: 新政策限制了公园允许的游客数量。

words.meaning_en: to compel or force; to restrict severely
words.derivation: con(一起) + strain(拉紧) → 拉紧约束住 → 限制
words.example: Budget cuts constrained the school's ability to hire new teachers.
words.example_zh: 预算削减限制了学校聘请新教师的能力。

words.meaning_en: to prevent someone from doing something; to hold back
words.derivation: re(回) + strain(拉紧) → 拉回来 → 抑制
words.example: Security guards had to restrain the unruly fan.
words.example_zh: 保安不得不制止那个不守规矩的粉丝。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + tig-(接触) + -uity(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the state of being in contact or near proximity
words.derivation: con(共同) + tig(接触) + uity(状态) → 共同接触的状态 → 邻近
words.example: The contiguity of the two nations led to significant cultural exchange.
words.example_zh: 两国的邻近关系促成了大量文化交流。

words.meaning_en: perceptible by touch; clear and definite
words.derivation: tang(触摸) + ible(能…的) → 能触摸到的 → 有形的
words.example: The project produced tangible results within the first year.
words.example_zh: 该项目在第一年就产生了切实的成果。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + tag-(接触) + -ion(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the communication of disease by direct or indirect contact
words.derivation: con(共同) + tag(接触) + ion(行为) → 共同接触 → 传染
words.example: Public health officials worked to prevent the contagion from spreading.
words.example_zh: 公共卫生官员努力防止疫情蔓延。

words.meaning_en: not damaged or impaired in any way; complete
words.derivation: in(未) + tact(触碰) → 未被触碰的 → 完整的
words.example: Remarkably, the ancient vase remained intact after the earthquake.
words.example_zh: 令人惊奇的是，这个古代花瓶在地震后仍然完好无损。

words.breakdown: con-(一起) + viv-(生活) + -ial(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: friendly, lively, and enjoyable; fond of feasting
words.derivation: con(一起) + viv(生活) + ial(…的) → 一起快乐生活的 → 欢乐的
words.example: The convivial atmosphere at the dinner party put everyone at ease.
words.example_zh: 晚宴上欢乐的气氛让每个人都感到轻松自在。

words.meaning_en: attractively lively and animated
words.derivation: viv(生活) + acious(充满…的) → 充满生命力的 → 活泼的
words.example: Her vivacious personality made her the center of attention at every party.
words.example_zh: 她活泼的性格使她在每个聚会上都成为关注的焦点。

words.meaning_en: the state of being strong and active; energy
words.derivation: vit(生命) + ality(性质) → 生命力的性质 → 活力
words.example: Regular exercise is essential for maintaining vitality as you age.
words.example_zh: 随着年龄增长，定期锻炼对保持活力至关重要。

words.meaning_en: to enliven or animate
words.derivation: viv(生命) + ify(使) → 使有生命 → 使活跃
words.example: Bright colors can vivify an otherwise dull room.
words.example_zh: 鲜艳的颜色能让原本沉闷的房间变得生动起来。

words.meaning_en: relating to a large company or group
words.derivation: corpor(身体) + ate(…的) → 形成一个整体的 → 公司的
words.example: The corporate headquarters is located in downtown Manhattan.
words.example_zh: 公司总部位于曼哈顿市中心。

words.breakdown: in-(进入) + corpor-(身体) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to include as part of a whole; to combine
words.derivation: in(进入) + corpor(身体) + ate → 纳入整体中 → 合并
words.example: The new design incorporates feedback from hundreds of users.
words.example_zh: 新设计融合了数百名用户的反馈。

words.meaning_en: having a large, bulky body; fat
words.derivation: corp(身体) + ulent(充满) → 身体充实的 → 肥胖的
words.example: The corpulent gentleman struggled to fit into the narrow airplane seat.
words.example_zh: 那位肥胖的绅士费力地挤进狭窄的飞机座位。

words.meaning_en: relating to a person's body; physical rather than spiritual
words.derivation: corpor(身体) + eal(…的) → 身体的 → 肉体的
words.example: Philosophers have long debated the relationship between corporeal and spiritual existence.
words.example_zh: 哲学家们长期以来一直在争论肉体存在与精神存在之间的关系。

words.meaning_en: relating to the universe or cosmos
words.derivation: cosm(宇宙) + ic(…的) → 宇宙的
words.example: Scientists study cosmic radiation to learn about the origins of the universe.
words.example_zh: 科学家研究宇宙辐射以了解宇宙的起源。

words.breakdown: cosm-(世界) + polit-(城市/公民) + -an(…的人)
words.meaning_en: familiar with and at ease in many different countries and cultures
words.derivation: cosm(世界) + polit(公民) + an → 世界公民 → 国际化的
