words.derivation: mandat(命令) + ory(…的) → 被命令的 → 强制的
words.example: Attendance at the safety training session is mandatory for all new employees.
words.example_zh: 所有新员工都必须参加安全培训课程。

words.meaning_en: an official order or commission to do something; authority given by voters
words.derivation: mand(命令) + ate → 所下的命令 → 授权
words.example: The president claimed a mandate from the voters to reform the healthcare system.
words.example_zh: 总统声称获得了选民的授权来改革医疗保健体系。

words.meaning_en: to send back into custody, especially while awaiting trial
words.derivation: re(回) + mand(命令) → 命令送回 → 还押
words.example: The judge remanded the suspect to custody pending further investigation.
words.example_zh: 法官将嫌疑人还押候审，等待进一步调查。

words.meaning_en: to revoke or cancel an order
words.derivation: counter(反) + mand(命令) → 下反命令 → 撤销命令
words.example: The general countermanded the original order and recalled the troops.
words.example_zh: 将军撤销了原来的命令，召回了部队。

words.breakdown: meta-(变化) + morph-(形状) + -ose(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to change completely in form or nature; to transform
words.derivation: meta(变化) + morph(形状) + ose → 改变形状 → 蜕变
words.example: The caterpillar metamorphosed into a beautiful butterfly over several weeks.
words.example_zh: 毛毛虫在几周内蜕变成了一只美丽的蝴蝶。

words.breakdown: a-(无) + morph-(形状) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: without a clearly defined shape or form
words.derivation: a(无) + morph(形状) + ous(…的) → 没有形状的 → 无定形的
words.example: The amorphous blob of clay was gradually shaped into an elegant vase.
words.example_zh: 那团无定形的黏土被逐渐塑造成一个精美的花瓶。

words.meaning_en: the study of the forms of things, especially organisms or words
words.derivation: morph(形态) + ology(学科) → 研究形态的学科 → 形态学
words.example: In linguistics, morphology examines how words are formed from smaller units of meaning.
words.example_zh: 在语言学中，形态学研究单词是如何由更小的意义单位构成的。

words.meaning_en: at the point of death; in terminal decline
words.derivation: mori(死亡) + bund(趋向) → 趋向死亡的 → 垂死的
words.example: The moribund industry was in desperate need of innovation to survive.
words.example_zh: 这个行将消亡的行业急需创新才能存活。

words.breakdown: mort-(死亡) + -al(形容词后缀) + -ity(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the state of being subject to death; death rate
words.meaning_zh: 死亡率；终有一死的命运
words.derivation: mort(死亡) + al(…的) + ity(状态) → 会死的状态 → 死亡率
words.example: Infant mortality rates have decreased dramatically due to advances in medicine.
words.example_zh: 由于医学的进步，婴儿死亡率已大幅下降。

words.breakdown: im-(不) + mort-(死亡) + -al(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: living forever; never forgotten
words.derivation: im(不) + mort(死亡) + al(…的) → 不会死的 → 不朽的
words.example: Shakespeare's works have made him immortal in the world of literature.
words.example_zh: 莎士比亚的作品使他在文学界永垂不朽。

words.meaning_en: to cause someone to feel embarrassed or humiliated
words.derivation: mort(死亡) + ify(使…) → 使人感觉要死了 → 使羞愧
words.example: She was mortified when she realized she had called her professor by the wrong name.
words.example_zh: 当她意识到自己叫错了教授的名字时，她感到无比尴尬。

words.meaning_en: characterized by an abnormal and unhealthy interest in disturbing subjects, especially death
words.derivation: morb(病/死) + id(…的) → 与病死相关的 → 病态的
words.example: His morbid fascination with horror films worried his parents.
words.example_zh: 他对恐怖电影的病态迷恋让他的父母很担心。

words.meaning_en: to undergo or cause a change in form or nature
words.derivation: mut(改变) + ate(使…) → 使改变 → 突变
words.example: Viruses can mutate rapidly, making it difficult to develop effective vaccines.
words.example_zh: 病毒可以迅速变异，使得开发有效疫苗变得困难。

words.meaning_en: to travel regularly between home and work; to reduce a judicial sentence
words.derivation: com(共同) + mut(改变) → 互相交换 → 通勤
words.example: Thousands of workers commute to the city center every morning by train.
words.example_zh: 每天早上有成千上万的工人乘火车通勤到市中心。

words.meaning_en: unchanging over time or unable to be changed
words.derivation: im(不) + mut(改变) + able(能…的) → 不能改变的 → 不可变的
words.example: The laws of physics are considered immutable across the universe.
words.example_zh: 物理定律被认为在整个宇宙中是不可改变的。

words.breakdown: per-(完全) + mut-(改变) + -ation(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: each of several possible ways in which things can be ordered or arranged
words.derivation: per(完全) + mut(改变) + ation(行为) → 完全改变排列 → 排列组合
words.example: The researchers tested every permutation of the variables to find the optimal solution.
words.example_zh: 研究人员测试了变量的每一种排列组合，以找到最优解。

words.meaning_en: just beginning to develop; emerging
words.derivation: nasc(出生) + ent(…的) → 刚出生的 → 新兴的
words.example: The nascent technology industry in the region attracted many young entrepreneurs.
words.example_zh: 该地区新兴的科技产业吸引了许多年轻的企业家。

words.meaning_en: inborn; natural rather than learned
words.derivation: in(内) + nat(出生) → 出生就在内部的 → 天生的
words.example: Researchers continue to debate whether language ability is innate or learned.
words.example_zh: 研究人员继续争论语言能力是天生的还是后天习得的。

words.meaning_en: a revival of or renewed interest in something; the great cultural period in Europe
words.derivation: re(再) + naiss(出生) + ance(状态) → 再次诞生 → 复兴
words.example: The city experienced a cultural renaissance after decades of economic decline.
words.example_zh: 这座城市在经历了数十年的经济衰退后，迎来了一次文化复兴。

words.meaning_en: relating to the place or time of one's birth
words.derivation: nat(出生) + al(…的) → 与出生相关的 → 出生的
words.example: Prenatal care is essential for the health of both mother and child.
words.example_zh: 产前护理对母亲和孩子的健康至关重要。

words.meaning_en: related by birth; having the same linguistic derivation
words.derivation: co(共同) + gnat(出生) → 共同出生的 → 同源的
words.example: English 'mother' and German 'Mutter' are cognate words derived from the same root.
words.example_zh: 英语的'mother'和德语的'Mutter'是来自同一词根的同源词。

words.meaning_en: of or concerning sailors or navigation; maritime
words.derivation: naut(航行) + ical(…的) → 与航行相关的 → 航海的
words.example: The museum displayed a collection of nautical instruments from the Age of Exploration.
words.example_zh: 博物馆展出了大航海时代的航海仪器收藏。

words.meaning_en: to plan and direct the course of a ship, aircraft, or journey
words.derivation: nav(船) + ig(驱动) + ate(使…) → 驱动船只 → 航行
words.example: Early explorers used the stars to navigate across vast oceans.
words.example_zh: 早期探险家利用星星在广阔的海洋上航行。

words.breakdown: circum-(环绕) + nav-(船) + -ig-(驱动) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to sail or travel all the way around something, especially the world
words.derivation: circum(环绕) + nav(船) + ig(驱动) + ate(使…) → 驾船环绕 → 环球航行
words.example: Magellan's expedition was the first to circumnavigate the globe.
words.example_zh: 麦哲伦的探险队是第一支环球航行的队伍。

words.meaning_en: a feeling of sickness with an inclination to vomit
words.derivation: naus(船) + ea → 坐船产生的不适感 → 恶心
words.example: The patient experienced severe nausea as a side effect of the medication.
words.example_zh: 患者因药物副作用而出现了严重的恶心。

words.meaning_en: to nullify; to make ineffective; to deny the existence of
words.derivation: neg(否定) + ate(使…) → 使否定 → 取消
words.example: The new evidence negated the earlier findings of the research team.
words.example_zh: 新证据否定了研究团队早期的发现。

words.breakdown: ab-(离开) + neg-(否定) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to renounce or reject; to give up a right or belief
words.derivation: ab(离开) + neg(否定) + ate(使…) → 否定并离开 → 放弃
words.example: The monk abnegated all material possessions to pursue a life of simplicity.
words.example_zh: 那位僧侣放弃了所有物质财产，追求简朴的生活。

words.meaning_en: to go back on a promise, undertaking, or contract
words.derivation: re(再) + neg(否定) → 再次否定 → 食言
words.example: The company reneged on its promise to provide additional funding for the project.
words.example_zh: 该公司违背了为项目提供额外资金的承诺。

words.meaning_en: a person who deserts a group, cause, or set of principles; a traitor
words.derivation: re(再) + neg(否定) + ade → 否定原来立场的人 → 叛徒
words.example: The renegade scientist published the classified research without authorization.
words.example_zh: 这个叛变的科学家未经授权便发表了机密研究成果。

words.meaning_en: done, occurring, or active at night
words.derivation: noct(夜晚) + urn + al(…的) → 属于夜晚的 → 夜间的
words.example: Owls are nocturnal creatures that hunt primarily after dark.
words.example_zh: 猫头鹰是夜行动物，主要在天黑后捕猎。

words.meaning_en: the time when the sun crosses the equator, making day and night approximately equal
words.meaning_zh: 昼夜平分点（春分或秋分）
words.derivation: equi(平等) + nox(夜晚) → 白天和夜晚等长 → 昼夜平分点
words.example: The vernal equinox marks the official beginning of spring in the Northern Hemisphere.
words.example_zh: 春分标志着北半球春天的正式开始。

words.meaning_en: harmful, poisonous, or very unpleasant
words.derivation: nox(有害) + ious(…的) → 有害的
words.example: The factory released noxious fumes that posed a serious health risk to nearby residents.
words.example_zh: 工厂排放的有害气体对附近居民的健康构成严重威胁。

words.breakdown: ob-(朝向) + nox-(有害) + -ious(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: extremely unpleasant or offensive
words.derivation: ob(朝向) + nox(有害) + ious(…的) → 面对有害的 → 令人讨厌的
words.example: His obnoxious behavior at the dinner party embarrassed everyone at the table.
words.example_zh: 他在晚宴上令人讨厌的行为让在场的每个人都感到尴尬。

words.breakdown: ob-(朝向) + sequ-(跟随) + -ious(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: obedient or attentive to an excessive degree; servile
words.derivation: ob(朝向) + sequ(跟随) + ious(…的) → 一直跟随的 → 谄媚的
words.example: The obsequious assistant agreed with everything the manager said.
words.example_zh: 那个谄媚的助理对经理说的每句话都表示赞同。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + secut-(跟随) + -ive(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: following each other without interruption
words.derivation: con(共同) + secut(跟随) + ive(…的) → 一个接一个跟随的 → 连续的
words.example: The team won five consecutive games to claim the championship title.
words.example_zh: 该队连赢五场比赛，夺得了冠军头衔。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + sequ-(跟随) + -ence(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a result or effect of an action or condition
words.derivation: con(共同) + sequ(跟随) + ence(状态) → 随之而来的 → 后果
words.example: Climate change will have severe consequences for coastal communities.
words.example_zh: 气候变化将对沿海社区产生严重的后果。

words.meaning_en: to institute legal proceedings against someone; to continue a course of action
words.derivation: pro(向前) + secut(跟随) → 一直追随到底 → 起诉
words.example: The district attorney decided to prosecute the case despite limited evidence.
words.example_zh: 尽管证据有限，地方检察官还是决定提起诉讼。

words.meaning_en: to subject someone to hostility and ill-treatment, especially because of their beliefs
words.derivation: per(贯穿) + secut(跟随) → 持续追踪 → 迫害
words.example: Throughout history, many groups have been persecuted for their religious beliefs.
words.example_zh: 纵观历史，许多群体因其宗教信仰而遭到迫害。

words.meaning_en: conforming to what is generally accepted as right or true; conventional
words.derivation: ortho(正确) + dox(观点) → 观点正确的 → 正统的
words.example: His orthodox approach to economics was challenged by newer theories.
words.example_zh: 他对经济学的正统观点受到了新理论的挑战。

words.breakdown: ortho-(正确) + graph-(书写) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the conventional spelling system of a language
words.derivation: ortho(正确) + graph(书写) + y → 正确的书写方式 → 正字法
words.example: English orthography is notoriously difficult because spelling often does not match pronunciation.
words.example_zh: 英语拼写法出了名的难，因为拼写往往与发音不一致。

words.breakdown: ortho-(正) + dont-(牙齿) + -ic(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: relating to the treatment of irregularities in the teeth and jaws
words.derivation: ortho(正) + dont(牙齿) + ic(…的) → 矫正牙齿的 → 正畸的
words.example: She wore braces for two years as part of her orthodontic treatment.
words.example_zh: 她戴了两年牙套，作为正畸治疗的一部分。

words.meaning_en: not conforming with accepted standards or beliefs
words.derivation: hetero(不同) + dox(观点) → 持不同观点的 → 非正统的
words.example: Her heterodox views on education reform made her unpopular among traditionalists.
words.example_zh: 她在教育改革方面的非正统观点使她在传统主义者中不受欢迎。

words.meaning_en: a formal written request, typically signed by many people, appealing to authority
words.derivation: pet(寻求) + ition(行为) → 寻求的行为 → 请愿
words.example: The citizens signed a petition urging the government to protect the local wetlands.
words.example_zh: 市民们签署了一份请愿书，敦促政府保护当地的湿地。

words.meaning_en: to strive to gain or win something by defeating others
words.derivation: com(共同) + pet(追求) → 共同追求 → 竞争
words.example: Students from thirty countries competed in the international science olympiad.
words.example_zh: 来自三十个国家的学生参加了国际科学奥林匹克竞赛。

words.breakdown: per-(贯穿) + pet-(追求) + -ual(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: never ending or changing; occurring repeatedly
words.derivation: per(贯穿) + pet(追求) + ual(…的) → 一直追求的 → 永久的
words.example: The region has been caught in a perpetual cycle of poverty and conflict.
words.example_zh: 该地区一直陷入贫困和冲突的永久循环中。

words.breakdown: im-(向内) + pet-(冲向) + -us(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the force or energy with which something moves; a driving force
words.derivation: im(向内) + pet(冲向) + us → 向内冲的力量 → 推动力
words.example: The discovery gave new impetus to research in renewable energy.
words.example_zh: 这一发现为可再生能源研究注入了新的推动力。

words.breakdown: centri-(中心) + pet-(寻求) + -al(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: moving or tending to move toward a center
words.derivation: centri(中心) + pet(寻求) + al(…的) → 寻求中心的 → 向心的
words.example: Centripetal force keeps the planets in orbit around the sun.
words.example_zh: 向心力使行星保持在围绕太阳的轨道上。

words.breakdown: phil-(爱) + anthrop-(人类) + -ic(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: seeking to promote the welfare of others; generous and benevolent
words.derivation: phil(爱) + anthrop(人类) + ic(…的) → 爱人类的 → 慈善的
words.example: The philanthropic foundation donated millions to improve education in rural areas.
words.example_zh: 该慈善基金会捐赠了数百万美元以改善农村地区的教育。

words.meaning_en: the study of the fundamental nature of knowledge, reality, and existence
words.derivation: philo(爱) + soph(智慧) + y → 爱智慧 → 哲学
words.example: Ancient Greek philosophy laid the groundwork for Western intellectual thought.
words.example_zh: 古希腊哲学为西方思想奠定了基础。

words.meaning_en: a person who collects or has a great love of books
words.derivation: biblio(书) + phil(爱) → 爱书的人 → 藏书家
words.example: As a bibliophile, she had amassed a personal library of over ten thousand volumes.
words.example_zh: 作为一个爱书之人，她已经积累了一万多册的私人藏书。

words.breakdown: phil-(爱) + harmon-(和谐) + -ic(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: devoted to music; used in the names of orchestras
words.meaning_zh: 爱好音乐的；交响乐团的
words.derivation: phil(爱) + harmon(和谐) + ic(…的) → 爱和谐之声的 → 爱好音乐的
words.example: The New York Philharmonic performed Beethoven's Ninth Symphony to a sold-out audience.
words.example_zh: 纽约爱乐乐团为满场观众演奏了贝多芬的第九交响曲。

words.meaning_en: to make someone less angry or hostile; to appease
words.derivation: plac(使平静) + ate(使…) → 使平静 → 安抚
words.example: The manager tried to placate the angry customers by offering a full refund.
words.example_zh: 经理试图通过提供全额退款来安抚愤怒的顾客。

words.meaning_en: not easily upset or excited; calm and peaceful
words.derivation: plac(平静) + id(…的) → 平静的
words.example: The placid lake reflected the surrounding mountains like a mirror.
words.example_zh: 平静的湖面像镜子一样倒映着周围的群山。

words.meaning_en: a harmless substance prescribed to reassure a patient; something with no real therapeutic effect
words.derivation: plac(使满意) + ebo → '我将使人满意' → 安慰剂
words.example: In the clinical trial, half the participants received the drug and half received a placebo.
words.example_zh: 在临床试验中，一半参与者服用了药物，另一半服用了安慰剂。

words.meaning_en: unable to be appeased or pacified; relentless
words.meaning_zh: 难以平息的，不可安抚的
words.derivation: im(不) + plac(安抚) + able(能…的) → 不能安抚的 → 难以平息的
words.example: The implacable opposition of the local community halted the construction project.
words.example_zh: 当地社区不可调和的反对使建设项目被迫停工。

words.meaning_en: filled or well-supplied with something
words.derivation: re(再次) + plet(充满) → 再次填满 → 充满的
words.example: The museum is replete with artifacts from ancient civilizations.
words.example_zh: 博物馆里充满了来自古代文明的文物。

words.meaning_en: to use up the supply or resources of
words.derivation: de(去除) + plet(充满) → 去除充满状态 → 耗尽
words.example: Overfishing has severely depleted the ocean's fish populations.
words.example_zh: 过度捕捞已严重耗尽了海洋的鱼类资源。

words.meaning_en: to fill something up again; to restore to a former level
words.derivation: re(再) + plen(充满) + ish(使…) → 再次使充满 → 补充
words.example: The government invested heavily to replenish the nation's strategic oil reserves.
words.example_zh: 政府大力投资以补充国家的战略石油储备。

words.meaning_en: to cause something to happen suddenly or prematurely; done hastily
words.derivation: pre(前) + cipit(头) + ate(使…) → 头朝前冲 → 促成，仓促
words.example: The economic crisis precipitated widespread social unrest across the region.
words.example_zh: 经济危机导致了该地区大范围的社会动荡。

words.meaning_en: dangerously high or steep; done suddenly and without careful consideration
words.derivation: precipit(急冲) + ous(…的) → 像头朝下冲的 → 陡峭的
words.example: The precipitous cliff made the hiking trail extremely dangerous.
words.example_zh: 陡峭的悬崖使这条徒步小径极其危险。

words.meaning_en: rain, snow, sleet, or hail that falls to the ground; the act of precipitating
words.derivation: precipit(急落) + ation(行为) → 急速落下的 → 降水
words.example: Annual precipitation in the desert region averages less than ten inches.
words.example_zh: 该沙漠地区的年降水量平均不足十英寸。

words.breakdown: pre-(提前) + coc-(成熟) + -ious(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: having developed certain abilities at an earlier age than usual
words.derivation: pre(提前) + coc(成熟) + ious(…的) → 提前成熟的 → 早熟的
words.example: The precocious child was reading novels by the age of five.
words.example_zh: 那个早慧的孩子五岁就在读小说了。

words.meaning_en: to make a dish or plan by combining ingredients; to devise a story or plan
words.derivation: con(一起) + coct(烹煮) → 将材料一起烹煮 → 调制，编造
words.example: She concocted an elaborate excuse for missing the deadline.
words.example_zh: 她编造了一个精心策划的借口来解释错过截止日期的原因。

words.meaning_en: the liquor resulting from concentrating the essence of a substance by boiling
words.derivation: de(下) + coct(烹煮) + ion(行为) → 煮下来的液体 → 煎液
words.example: Traditional medicine often relies on herbal decoctions to treat common ailments.
words.example_zh: 传统医学常常依赖草药煎剂来治疗常见疾病。

words.meaning_en: relating to a mother; motherly
words.derivation: mater(母亲) + nal(…的) → 属于母亲的 → 母性的
words.example: Her maternal instincts compelled her to comfort the crying child.
words.example_zh: 她的母性本能驱使她去安慰那个哭泣的孩子。

words.meaning_en: a social system in which the mother is head of the family
words.derivation: matri(母亲) + archy(统治) → 母亲统治 → 母权制
words.example: Some anthropologists have studied matriarchy in certain indigenous cultures.
words.example_zh: 一些人类学家研究了某些原住民文化中的母系社会。

words.breakdown: metro-(母) + polit-(城市) + -an(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: relating to a large city and its surrounding areas
words.derivation: metro(母) + polit(城市) + an(…的) → 母城的 → 大都市的
words.example: The metropolitan area has a population of over five million people.
words.example_zh: 该大都市地区拥有超过五百万人口。

words.meaning_en: devotion to and vigorous support for one's country
words.derivation: patri(祖国) + ot(人) + ism(主义) → 爱祖国的主义 → 爱国主义
words.example: The speech inspired a deep sense of patriotism among the audience.
words.example_zh: 这篇演讲在听众中激发了深深的爱国情怀。

words.breakdown: ex-(出) + patri-(祖国) + -ate(名词/动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a person who lives outside their native country
words.derivation: ex(出) + patri(祖国) + ate → 离开祖国的人 → 侨民
words.example: Many expatriates living abroad maintain strong ties with their home country.
words.example_zh: 许多居住在海外的侨民与自己的祖国保持着密切的联系。

words.meaning_en: to send someone back to their own country
words.derivation: re(回) + patri(祖国) + ate(使…) → 使回到祖国 → 遣返
words.example: The government agreed to repatriate the refugees once conditions improved.
words.example_zh: 政府同意在条件改善后遣返难民。

words.meaning_en: relating to a father; fatherly
words.derivation: pater(父亲) + nal(…的) → 属于父亲的 → 父亲的
words.example: His paternal grandfather emigrated from Italy in the early twentieth century.
words.example_zh: 他的祖父在二十世纪初从意大利移民过来。

words.meaning_en: a fellow citizen or national of a country
words.derivation: com(共同) + patri(祖国) + ot(人) → 来自同一祖国的人 → 同胞
words.example: She met several compatriots while studying at the university abroad.
words.example_zh: 她在国外大学读书时遇到了几位同胞。

words.meaning_en: exhibiting extremely wild or violent behavior; insane
words.derivation: mania(疯狂) + ac + al(…的) → 像疯了一样的 → 疯狂的
words.example: The villain let out a maniacal laugh as the building collapsed.
words.example_zh: 大楼倒塌时，那个恶棍发出了疯狂的笑声。

words.meaning_en: obsession with the exercise of power; delusion of grandeur
words.derivation: megalo(巨大) + mania(疯狂) → 对伟大的疯狂追求 → 妄自尊大
words.example: The dictator's megalomania led him to build monuments in his own honor.
words.example_zh: 独裁者的妄自尊大促使他为自己建造纪念碑。

words.meaning_en: a recurrent urge to steal, typically without regard for need or profit
words.derivation: klepto(偷盗) + mania(疯狂) → 对偷盗的疯狂冲动 → 盗窃癖
words.example: Kleptomania is a recognized psychological disorder that requires professional treatment.
words.example_zh: 盗窃癖是一种公认的心理疾病，需要专业治疗。

words.meaning_en: a deep, pensive, and long-lasting sadness
words.derivation: melan(黑) + chol(胆汁) + y → 古希腊人认为黑胆汁过多导致忧郁 → 忧郁
words.example: A sense of melancholy pervaded the abandoned village.
words.example_zh: 一种忧郁的气息弥漫在这个被遗弃的村庄中。
words.mnemonic: melan(黑) + choly(胆汁)：古人认为体内黑色胆汁多就会忧郁

words.meaning_en: dark or dull in color or tone; gloomy
words.derivation: 法语 sombre(阴暗) → 气氛阴沉 → 忧郁的
words.example: The somber mood at the memorial service reflected the community's grief.
words.example_zh: 追悼会上忧郁的气氛反映了整个社区的悲伤。
words.mnemonic: somber 听起来像'桑拿伯'：在阴暗的桑拿房里，气氛总是很沉闷

words.meaning_en: looking or sounding sad and dismal
words.derivation: 拉丁语 lugere(哀悼) → 充满哀悼的 → 悲哀的
words.example: The lugubrious music added to the film's atmosphere of despair.
words.example_zh: 那悲哀的音乐加深了影片中绝望的氛围。
words.mnemonic: lu(路) + gubr(哭) + ious：走在路上一边哭一边走 → 悲哀的

words.meaning_en: an area of territory owned or controlled by a ruler; a field of activity or knowledge
words.derivation: dom(统治) + ain → 统治的范围 → 领域
words.example: Climate change research spans multiple scientific domains.
words.example_zh: 气候变化研究横跨多个科学领域。

words.meaning_en: the country that a person treats as their permanent home; a person's residence
words.derivation: dom(房屋) + icile(小) → 住的小屋 → 住所
words.example: For tax purposes, her legal domicile is still in her home state.
words.example_zh: 出于税收目的，她的法定住所仍在她的家乡州。

words.meaning_en: to tame an animal or cultivate a plant for human use
words.derivation: dom(房屋) + estic(属于) + ate(使) → 使属于家庭 → 驯化
words.example: Humans first domesticated dogs over 15,000 years ago.
words.example_zh: 人类在一万五千多年前首次驯化了狗。

words.breakdown: domin-(统治) + -eer(做…的人) + -ing(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: asserting one's will over another in an arrogant way
words.derivation: domin(统治) + eer(做) + ing → 像统治者一样行事的 → 专横的
words.example: The domineering manager stifled creativity among the team members.
words.example_zh: 那位专横的经理扼杀了团队成员的创造力。

words.breakdown: pre-(在前) + domin-(统治) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to be the strongest or main element; to have control or power over
words.derivation: pre(在前) + domin(统治) + ate → 在前面统治 → 占优势
words.example: Coniferous forests predominate in the northern regions of the continent.
words.example_zh: 针叶林在该大陆北部地区占主导地位。

words.breakdown: pre-(提前) + monit-(警告) + -ion(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a strong feeling that something is about to happen, especially something unpleasant
words.derivation: pre(提前) + monit(警告) + ion → 提前的警告 → 预感
words.example: She had a premonition that something terrible would happen during the expedition.
words.example_zh: 她有一种预感，觉得这次探险中会发生可怕的事。

words.breakdown: ad-(朝向) + mon-(警告) + -ish(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to warn or reprimand someone firmly
words.derivation: ad(朝向) + mon(警告) + ish → 对着某人警告 → 告诫
words.example: The teacher admonished the students for not completing their assignments on time.
words.example_zh: 老师告诫学生们没有按时完成作业。

words.meaning_en: to authoritatively call on someone to be present; to urgently demand
words.derivation: sum(在下面) + mon(提醒) → 暗中提醒前来 → 召唤
words.example: The committee summoned experts to testify about the environmental impact.
words.example_zh: 委员会传唤专家就环境影响作证。

words.breakdown: pre-(提前) + sumpt-(拿取) + -uous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: failing to observe the limits of what is appropriate; excessively bold
words.derivation: pre(提前) + sumpt(拿) + uous → 提前就拿了 → 自以为是的、放肆的
words.example: It would be presumptuous to assume we can solve this problem without consulting the experts.
words.example_zh: 不咨询专家就假设我们能解决这个问题是很冒昧的。

words.meaning_en: to suppose to be the case without proof; to take on a responsibility
words.derivation: as(朝向) + sume(拿) → 拿来当真 → 假设；拿过来 → 承担
words.example: Scientists should not assume that correlation implies causation.
words.example_zh: 科学家不应该假设相关性意味着因果关系。

words.breakdown: con-(完全) + sumpt-(拿取) + -ion(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the using up of a resource; the eating or drinking of something
words.derivation: con(完全) + sumpt(拿) + ion → 全部拿走 → 消耗
words.example: The country's energy consumption has doubled over the past two decades.
words.example_zh: 该国的能源消耗在过去二十年中翻了一番。

words.breakdown: proli-(后代) + fer-(携带) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to increase rapidly in numbers; to multiply
words.derivation: proli(后代) + fer(携带) + ate → 不断产生后代 → 激增
words.example: Social media platforms have proliferated over the past decade.
words.example_zh: 社交媒体平台在过去十年中激增。

words.meaning_en: producing much fruit or many offspring; highly productive
words.derivation: proli(后代) + fic(做) → 不断产生后代的 → 多产的
words.example: The prolific author published over forty novels during her career.
words.example_zh: 这位多产的作家在其职业生涯中出版了四十多部小说。

words.breakdown: pro-(向前) + pens-(悬挂，倾斜) + -ity(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: an inclination or natural tendency to behave in a particular way
words.derivation: pro(向前) + pens(悬挂) + ity → 向前倾斜 → 倾向
words.example: Adolescents have a propensity for risk-taking behavior.
words.example_zh: 青少年有冒险行为的倾向。

words.meaning_en: to temporarily prevent from continuing; to hang from above
words.derivation: sus(在下面) + pend(悬挂) → 悬在下方 → 悬挂；使悬而不决 → 暂停
words.example: The university decided to suspend the research program pending further review.
words.example_zh: 大学决定暂停该研究项目，等待进一步审查。

words.meaning_en: to add something as an attachment to the end of a document
words.derivation: ap(朝向) + pend(悬挂) → 挂在旁边 → 附加
words.example: The researcher appended a glossary of technical terms to the report.
words.example_zh: 研究者在报告后面附加了一份技术术语表。

words.breakdown: im-(在上方) + pend-(悬挂) + -ing(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: about to happen; imminent
words.derivation: im(在上方) + pend(悬挂) + ing → 悬在头上的 → 即将发生的
words.example: Scientists warned of the impending ecological crisis caused by deforestation.
words.example_zh: 科学家们警告了森林砍伐导致的即将来临的生态危机。

words.breakdown: re-(回) + com-(共同) + pens-(称量，支付)
words.meaning_en: to make amends to someone for loss or harm; compensation
words.derivation: re(回) + com(共同) + pens(支付) → 回头支付 → 赔偿
words.example: The court ordered the company to provide recompense for damages caused by the oil spill.
words.example_zh: 法院责令该公司对石油泄漏造成的损害进行赔偿。

words.meaning_en: eager or quick to argue, quarrel, or fight
words.derivation: pugn(战斗) + acious(倾向于) → 倾向于战斗的 → 好斗的
words.example: The pugnacious politician was known for his combative style in debates.
words.example_zh: 这位好斗的政治家以辩论中的好战风格著称。

words.breakdown: re-(回) + pugn-(战斗) + -ant(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: extremely distasteful; unacceptable
words.meaning_zh: 令人反感的，令人厌恶的
words.derivation: re(回) + pugn(战斗) + ant → 让人想打回去的 → 令人反感的
words.example: Many people find the idea of animal testing morally repugnant.
words.example_zh: 很多人认为动物实验在道德上令人反感。

words.meaning_en: to dispute the truth, validity, or honesty of a statement or motive
words.derivation: im(向内) + pugn(战斗) → 向某人开战 → 抨击
words.example: The defense attorney tried to impugn the credibility of the witness.
words.example_zh: 辩护律师试图质疑证人的可信度。

words.meaning_en: showing great attention to detail or correct behavior
words.meaning_zh: 一丝不苟的，拘泥细节的
words.derivation: punct(点) + ilious → 每一点都注意到的 → 一丝不苟的
words.example: The punctilious editor caught every typographical error in the manuscript.
words.example_zh: 那位一丝不苟的编辑发现了手稿中的每一个排版错误。

words.meaning_en: a small hole made by a sharp object; to make such a hole
words.derivation: punct(刺) + ure → 刺出来的 → 穿刺
words.example: A nail punctured the tire, causing it to deflate slowly.
words.example_zh: 一根钉子刺穿了轮胎，导致它慢慢漏气。

words.meaning_en: a feeling of guilt or moral scruple that prevents wrongdoing
words.derivation: com(加强) + punct(刺) + ion → 良心被刺痛 → 内疚
words.example: He felt no compunction about firing employees who consistently underperformed.
words.example_zh: 他对解雇持续表现不佳的员工毫无内疚。

words.meaning_en: having a sharply strong taste or smell
words.derivation: pung(刺) + ent → 刺鼻的 → 辛辣的
words.example: The pungent aroma of garlic filled the kitchen.
words.example_zh: 刺鼻的大蒜味弥漫了整个厨房。

words.meaning_en: to erase or remove completely
words.derivation: ex(出) + pung(刺) → 用尖物刮出去 → 抹去
words.example: The court agreed to expunge the minor offense from his criminal record.
words.example_zh: 法院同意从他的犯罪记录中删除这一轻微犯罪。

words.meaning_en: a fictitious name, especially used by an author
words.derivation: pseudo(假的) + nym(名字) → 假名字 → 笔名
words.example: Many female writers in the 19th century published under male pseudonyms.
words.example_zh: 19世纪许多女作家用男性笔名出版作品。

words.breakdown: an-(没有) + onym-(名字) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: not identified by name; of unknown authorship
words.derivation: an(没有) + onym(名字) + ous → 没有名字的 → 匿名的
words.example: The donation was made by an anonymous benefactor.
words.example_zh: 这笔捐款由一位匿名捐助者提供。

words.meaning_en: to formally propose someone as a candidate for a position or award
words.derivation: nomin(名字) + ate → 给出名字 → 提名
words.example: The committee nominated three candidates for the leadership position.
words.example_zh: 委员会为领导职位提名了三位候选人。

words.breakdown: ig-(否定) + nomin-(名字) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: public shame or disgrace
words.derivation: ig(否定) + nomin(名字) + y → 失去好名声 → 耻辱
words.example: The politician resigned in ignominy after the scandal was exposed.
words.example_zh: 丑闻曝光后，这位政治家在耻辱中辞职。

words.meaning_en: aggressively greedy or grasping
words.derivation: rap(抢夺) + acious(倾向于) → 倾向于抢夺的 → 贪婪的
words.example: The rapacious logging industry devastated vast stretches of tropical rainforest.
words.example_zh: 贪婪的伐木业摧毁了大片热带雨林。

words.meaning_en: a feeling of intense pleasure or joy
words.derivation: rapt(攫取) + ure → 心灵被攫走 → 狂喜
words.example: The audience listened in rapture as the orchestra performed the symphony.
words.example_zh: 观众如痴如醉地聆听管弦乐团演奏交响曲。

words.meaning_en: completely fascinated by what one is seeing or hearing
words.derivation: rapt(被攫走的) → 心神被攫走 → 全神贯注的
words.example: The students sat in rapt attention during the guest lecturer's presentation.
words.example_zh: 学生们在客座讲师的演讲中全神贯注。

words.meaning_en: to cause severe and extensive damage to
words.derivation: rav(抢夺) + age → 大肆掠夺 → 肆虐
words.example: The wildfire ravaged thousands of acres of forest before it was contained.
words.example_zh: 野火在被控制之前肆虐了数千英亩的森林。

words.meaning_en: extremely hungry; voracious
words.derivation: rav(抢夺) + enous → 像要抢食一样的 → 极度饥饿的
words.example: After the ten-mile hike, the hikers were ravenous and devoured their meals.
words.example_zh: 十英里的徒步之后，徒步者们饥肠辘辘，狼吞虎咽地吃完了饭。

words.meaning_en: based on or in accordance with reason or logic
words.derivation: ratio(理性) + nal → 基于理性的 → 合理的
words.example: Scientists seek rational explanations for natural phenomena.
words.example_zh: 科学家为自然现象寻求合理的解释。

words.meaning_en: to sign or give formal consent to a treaty or agreement, making it officially valid
words.derivation: rat(确定) + ify(使) → 使确定 → 正式批准
words.example: All member nations must ratify the treaty before it takes effect.
words.example_zh: 所有成员国必须批准该条约后，条约才能生效。

words.meaning_en: the process of logical reasoning
words.derivation: ratio(理性) + cination → 运用理性的过程 → 推理
words.example: The detective's ratiocination led him to identify the true culprit.
words.example_zh: 侦探的推理使他找到了真正的罪犯。

words.meaning_en: a government or system, especially an authoritarian one; a systematic plan
words.derivation: reg(统治) + ime → 统治体系 → 政权
words.example: The authoritarian regime suppressed freedom of the press for decades.
words.example_zh: 这个专制政权压制新闻自由长达数十年。

words.meaning_en: to control or supervise by means of rules and regulations
words.derivation: reg(规则) + ul + ate → 按规则来 → 调节
words.example: The government regulates carbon emissions to combat climate change.
words.example_zh: 政府管控碳排放以应对气候变化。

words.meaning_en: extremely thorough, exhaustive, or accurate
words.derivation: rig(严格) + orous → 严格到极致的 → 严谨的
words.example: The study was praised for its rigorous methodology and comprehensive data analysis.
words.example_zh: 这项研究因其严谨的方法论和全面的数据分析而受到赞誉。

words.meaning_en: unable to bend or be forced out of shape; strict and unyielding
words.derivation: rig(直的) + id → 直挺挺的 → 僵硬的，严格的
words.example: The rigid social hierarchy limited opportunities for upward mobility.
words.example_zh: 僵化的社会等级制度限制了向上流动的机会。

words.meaning_en: to move in a circular orbit; to center on
words.derivation: re(反复) + volv(旋转) → 反复旋转 → 围绕
words.example: The Earth revolves around the Sun once every 365 days.
words.example_zh: 地球每365天围绕太阳旋转一周。

words.meaning_en: to develop gradually, especially from a simple to a more complex form
words.derivation: e(出) + volv(滚动) → 展开滚动 → 逐步发展 → 进化
words.example: Species evolve through the process of natural selection over millions of years.
words.example_zh: 物种通过数百万年的自然选择过程进化。

words.meaning_en: to rise in rebellion; to feel strong disgust
words.derivation: re(反) + volt(转) → 反转立场 → 反叛
words.example: The colonists revolted against the imperial government's oppressive taxation policies.
words.example_zh: 殖民者反抗帝国政府的压迫性税收政策。

words.breakdown: con-(一起) + volut-(旋转) + -ed(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: extremely complex and difficult to follow
words.derivation: con(一起) + volut(旋转) + ed → 绕在一起的 → 错综复杂的
words.example: The professor's convoluted explanation left the students more confused than before.
words.example_zh: 教授费解的解释让学生们比之前更加困惑。

words.meaning_en: speaking or spoken incessantly and fluently
words.derivation: vol(旋转) + uble → 嘴巴不停转动的 → 健谈的
words.example: The voluble tour guide kept the group entertained with stories throughout the trip.
words.example_zh: 那位健谈的导游在整个旅途中用故事让大家开心不已。

words.meaning_en: plump; round and spherical
words.derivation: rot(圆) + und → 圆形的 → 圆胖的
words.example: The rotund ceramic vases from the Tang Dynasty are prized by collectors.
words.example_zh: 唐代浑圆的陶瓷花瓶深受收藏家珍视。

words.meaning_en: to move or cause to move in a circle around an axis
words.derivation: rot(转动) + ate → 使转动 → 旋转
words.example: Farmers rotate crops to maintain soil fertility.
words.example_zh: 农民轮作农作物以保持土壤肥力。

words.meaning_en: a profoundly wise person; wise and judicious
words.derivation: sag(智慧) → 有智慧的 → 圣人
words.example: The community looked to the sage elder for guidance during times of crisis.
words.example_zh: 社区在危机时期向这位睿智的长者寻求指导。

words.meaning_en: having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgment
words.derivation: sag(智慧) + acious → 充满智慧的 → 睿智的
words.example: The sagacious investor predicted the market downturn months in advance.
words.example_zh: 这位精明的投资者提前数月预测到了市场下跌。

words.meaning_en: to be a sign or warning that something will happen; an omen
words.derivation: pre(提前) + sag(感知) → 提前感知到 → 预示
words.example: Dark clouds presaged the arrival of a severe thunderstorm.
words.example_zh: 乌云预示着一场严重雷暴的到来。

words.breakdown: in-(没有) + sip-(品尝) + -id(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: lacking flavor or interest; dull
words.derivation: in(没有) + sip(品尝) + id → 没有味道的 → 乏味的
words.example: Critics dismissed the film as insipid and lacking originality.
words.example_zh: 评论家认为这部电影平淡乏味，缺乏创意。

words.meaning_en: a place of refuge or safety; a nature reserve
words.derivation: sanct(神圣) + uary(地方) → 神圣之地 → 避难所
words.example: The wildlife sanctuary protects endangered species from poaching.
words.example_zh: 这个野生动物保护区保护濒危物种免受偷猎。

words.meaning_en: official permission or approval; a penalty for disobeying a law
words.derivation: sanct(神圣) + ion → 神圣的认可 → 批准；神圣的惩罚 → 制裁
words.example: The United Nations imposed economic sanctions on the country for violating international law.
words.example_zh: 联合国因该国违反国际法而对其实施经济制裁。

words.meaning_en: regarded as too important or valuable to be interfered with
words.derivation: sacro(神圣) + sanct(神圣) → 双重神圣 → 神圣不可侵犯的
words.example: Freedom of speech is considered sacrosanct in democratic societies.
words.example_zh: 言论自由在民主社会中被视为神圣不可侵犯的。

words.breakdown: con-(共同，加强) + secr-(神圣) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to make or declare sacred; to dedicate formally to a religious purpose
words.derivation: con(加强) + secr(神圣) + ate → 使变得神圣 → 奉献
words.example: The cathedral was consecrated in a grand ceremony attended by thousands.
words.example_zh: 大教堂在一场数千人参加的盛大仪式中被祝圣。

words.breakdown: de-(去除) + secr-(神圣) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to treat a sacred place or thing with violent disrespect
words.derivation: de(去除) + secr(神圣) + ate → 去除神圣 → 亵渎
words.example: Vandals desecrated the ancient temple by spray-painting graffiti on its walls.
words.example_zh: 破坏者在古庙的墙上喷涂涂鸦，亵渎了这座神殿。

words.meaning_en: relating to earthquakes; having enormous effect
words.derivation: seism(地震) + ic → 地震的 → 震动性的
words.example: The discovery of DNA caused a seismic shift in the field of biology.
words.example_zh: DNA的发现在生物学领域引起了巨大的变革。

words.meaning_en: the branch of science concerned with earthquakes
words.derivation: seism(地震) + ology(学科) → 研究地震的学科 → 地震学
words.example: Advances in seismology have improved our ability to predict volcanic eruptions.
words.example_zh: 地震学的进步提高了我们预测火山爆发的能力。

words.meaning_en: infected with bacteria; relating to sepsis
words.derivation: sept(腐败) + ic → 腐败的 → 感染的
words.example: The wound became septic because it was not properly cleaned.
words.example_zh: 伤口因未得到适当清洁而感染。

words.breakdown: anti-(反对) + sept-(腐败) + -ic(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a substance that prevents infection by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms
words.derivation: anti(反对) + sept(腐败) + ic → 反腐败的 → 防腐的
words.example: Joseph Lister pioneered the use of antiseptic techniques in surgical procedures.
words.example_zh: 约瑟夫·李斯特开创了在外科手术中使用消毒技术。

words.breakdown: a-(没有) + sept-(腐败) + -ic(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: free from contamination caused by harmful bacteria or other microorganisms
words.derivation: a(没有) + sept(腐败) + ic → 没有腐败的 → 无菌的
words.example: Aseptic conditions in the laboratory are essential for cell culture experiments.
words.example_zh: 实验室中的无菌条件对细胞培养实验至关重要。

words.breakdown: solid-(坚固的，团结的) + -arity(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: unity or agreement of feeling or action among individuals with a common interest
words.derivation: solid(坚固) + arity → 坚固的联合 → 团结
words.example: Workers showed solidarity by refusing to cross the picket line.
words.example_zh: 工人们通过拒绝穿越纠察线表示团结。

words.meaning_en: comfort or consolation in a time of distress or sadness
words.derivation: sol(安慰) → 得到宽慰 → 慰藉
words.example: She found solace in music after the loss of her grandmother.
words.example_zh: 失去祖母后，她在音乐中找到了慰藉。

words.meaning_en: to comfort someone at a time of grief or disappointment
words.derivation: con(加强) + sol(安慰) → 加强安慰 → 慰问
words.example: Friends gathered to console the family after the tragic accident.
words.example_zh: 悲剧事故发生后，朋友们聚在一起安慰这个家庭。

words.breakdown: in-(不能) + con-(加强) + sol-(安慰) + -able(能…的)
words.meaning_en: not able to be comforted or consoled
words.meaning_zh: 无法安慰的，极度悲伤的
words.derivation: in(不能) + consolable(可安慰的) → 无法安慰的
words.example: The child was inconsolable after losing her beloved pet.
words.example_zh: 孩子失去心爱的宠物后悲痛欲绝。

words.meaning_en: diligent, thorough, and extremely attentive to details
words.derivation: scrup(顾虑) + ulous → 充满顾虑的 → 一丝不苟的
words.example: The researcher was scrupulous in documenting every step of the experiment.
words.example_zh: 研究人员一丝不苟地记录了实验的每一步。

words.meaning_en: to examine or inspect closely and thoroughly
words.derivation: scrut(检查) + inize → 进行检查 → 仔细审视
words.example: The auditors scrutinized the company's financial records for irregularities.
words.example_zh: 审计员仔细审查了公司的财务记录以查找违规行为。

words.meaning_en: impossible to understand or interpret
words.meaning_zh: 高深莫测的，难以理解的
words.derivation: in(不能) + scrut(检查) + able → 无法看透的 → 高深莫测的
words.example: The diplomat maintained an inscrutable expression throughout the negotiations.
words.example_zh: 外交官在整个谈判过程中保持着高深莫测的表情。

words.meaning_en: the quality of being deep, full, and reverberating; the power to evoke images or emotions
words.derivation: re(回) + son(声音) + ance → 声音回响 → 共鸣
words.example: The speaker's message had deep resonance with the audience.
words.example_zh: 演讲者的信息引起了听众的深刻共鸣。

words.breakdown: dis-(分离) + son-(声音) + -ance(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a lack of harmony; a tension resulting from conflicting elements
words.derivation: dis(分离) + son(声音) + ance → 声音分离的 → 不和谐
words.example: Cognitive dissonance occurs when people hold contradictory beliefs simultaneously.
words.example_zh: 当人们同时持有矛盾的信念时，就会产生认知失调。

words.breakdown: con-(一起) + son-(声音) + -ance(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: agreement or compatibility between opinions or actions; harmonious sound
words.derivation: con(一起) + son(声音) + ance → 声音合在一起 → 和谐
words.example: The policy must be in consonance with the organization's core values.
words.example_zh: 该政策必须与组织的核心价值观保持一致。

words.meaning_en: the mental action or process of acquiring knowledge through thought and experience
words.derivation: cogn(知道) + ition → 知道的过程 → 认知
words.example: Research in cognition has revealed how memory and attention interact.
words.example_zh: 认知研究揭示了记忆和注意力如何相互作用。

words.meaning_en: having knowledge or being aware of
words.derivation: cogn(知道) + izant → 知道的 → 有意识的
words.example: Investors should be cognizant of the risks associated with volatile markets.
words.example_zh: 投资者应该意识到与波动市场相关的风险。

words.breakdown: re-(再次) + cogn-(知道) + -ize(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to identify someone or something from having encountered them before
words.derivation: re(再次) + cogn(知道) + ize → 再次知道 → 认出
words.example: The scientific community finally recognized her contributions to the field of genetics.
words.example_zh: 科学界终于承认了她对遗传学领域的贡献。

words.meaning_en: to formally forbid by law, rule, or authority
words.derivation: pro(在前) + hibit(持有) → 提前持有否决权 → 禁止
words.example: The new legislation prohibits the use of single-use plastics in public spaces.
words.example_zh: 新立法禁止在公共场所使用一次性塑料制品。

words.meaning_en: to publicly display in a gallery or museum; to show a quality or behavior
words.derivation: ex(出) + hibit(持有) → 拿出来给人看 → 展览
words.example: The museum will exhibit artifacts from ancient Egyptian tombs.
words.example_zh: 博物馆将展出来自古埃及墓葬的文物。

words.meaning_en: to hinder, restrain, or prevent an action or process
words.derivation: in(在内) + hibit(持有) → 把东西控制在内 → 抑制
words.example: Certain chemicals in the brain inhibit the transmission of pain signals.
words.example_zh: 大脑中的某些化学物质会抑制疼痛信号的传递。

words.meaning_en: to spread and promote an idea or breed organisms
words.derivation: propag(传播) + ate → 使传播 → 散布
words.mnemonic: propagate和propaganda(宣传)同源→传播信息→繁殖也是「传播」基因
words.example: Plants propagate through seeds, cuttings, or other reproductive methods.
words.example_zh: 植物通过种子、扦插或其他繁殖方式传播。

words.meaning_en: information used to promote a political cause or point of view, often biased or misleading
words.derivation: propag(传播) + anda → 应被传播的东西 → 宣传
words.mnemonic: propaganda来自拉丁语「应当传播的」→有目的的宣传→带有偏见的信息传播
words.example: During the war, both sides used propaganda to boost morale and vilify the enemy.
words.example_zh: 战争期间，双方都利用宣传来鼓舞士气并丑化敌人。

words.breakdown: pro-(向前) + mulg-(公众) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to make widely known; to put a law into effect by official proclamation
words.derivation: pro(向前) + mulg(公众) + ate → 向公众宣布 → 颁布
words.mnemonic: pro(向前)+mulgate→向公众宣告→颁布；与propagate一组记，都含「向外传播」之意
words.example: The government promulgated new regulations to address cybersecurity threats.
words.example_zh: 政府颁布了新的法规来应对网络安全威胁。

words.meaning_en: having an obstinately uncooperative attitude toward authority
words.derivation: re(回) + calcitr(踢) + ant → 踢回去 → 不服从的
words.mnemonic: re(回)+calcitr(踢，联想calcium钙，坚硬)+ant→硬踢回去→顽固不化的
words.example: The recalcitrant student refused to follow the school's dress code.
words.example_zh: 那个顽固不化的学生拒绝遵守学校的着装规定。

words.breakdown: re-(反) + bell-(战争) + -ious(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: showing a desire to resist authority, control, or convention
words.derivation: re(反) + bell(战争) + ious → 反战的 → 叛逆的
words.mnemonic: rebel(反叛)+ious→叛逆的；与recalcitrant配对记忆，都表示不服从
words.example: The rebellious colonists organized protests against the imperial tax policies.
words.example_zh: 叛逆的殖民者组织了反对帝国税收政策的抗议。

words.meaning_en: to respond to a gesture or action by making a corresponding one
words.derivation: reciproc(互相) + ate → 互相来回 → 回报
words.mnemonic: reciproc(来回)+ate→来回交换→互相回报；reciprocal(倒数)也是「反过来」之意
words.example: If we show goodwill toward our neighbors, they are likely to reciprocate.
words.example_zh: 如果我们对邻居表示善意，他们很可能会回报。

words.meaning_en: the practice of exchanging things with others for mutual benefit
words.derivation: reciproc(互相) + ity → 互相交换的状态 → 互惠
words.mnemonic: reciproc(来回)+ity→来回交换的状态→互惠原则；贸易谈判中的核心概念
words.example: International trade agreements are built on the principle of reciprocity.
words.example_zh: 国际贸易协议建立在互惠原则的基础上。

words.meaning_en: formidable, especially as an opponent; arousing fear or respect
words.derivation: re(加强) + doubt(畏惧) + able → 令人非常畏惧的 → 令人敬畏的
words.mnemonic: redoubtable中的doubt不是「怀疑」而是古法语的「畏惧」→令人生畏的对手→formidable的同义词
words.example: She proved to be a redoubtable competitor, winning every debate with ease.
words.example_zh: 她证明了自己是一位令人敬畏的竞争者，轻松赢得了每场辩论。

words.meaning_en: inspiring fear or respect through being impressively large or powerful
words.derivation: formid(可怕的) + able → 令人害怕的 → 强大的
words.mnemonic: formidable→form(形态)+id+able→光看形态就让人害怕→强大的；与redoubtable配对记
words.example: The team faces a formidable challenge in qualifying for the international tournament.
words.example_zh: 该队在获得国际锦标赛资格方面面临着严峻的挑战。

words.meaning_en: occurring at irregular intervals or only in a few places
words.derivation: sporad(散布) + ic → 散布各处的 → 零星的
words.mnemonic: sporadic中spor-联想spore(孢子)→孢子随风零星散落→sporadic偶发的、零星的
words.example: Sporadic outbreaks of the disease occurred in remote villages throughout the region.
words.example_zh: 该地区的偏远村庄零星暴发了这种疾病。

words.meaning_en: to pull or twist out of shape; to give a misleading account of
words.derivation: dis(分离) + tort(扭) → 向不同方向扭 → 扭曲
words.example: The media can distort the facts to fit a particular narrative.
words.example_zh: 媒体可能会歪曲事实以迎合某种叙事。

words.meaning_en: to twist or bend out of normal shape
words.derivation: con(一起) + tort(扭) → 一起扭 → 严重变形
words.example: The dancer contorted her body into impossible positions.
words.example_zh: 那位舞者将身体扭成了不可思议的姿势。

words.meaning_en: to obtain something by force, threats, or other unfair means
words.derivation: ex(出) + tort(扭) → 扭出来 → 强行榨取 → 勒索
words.example: The criminals attempted to extort money from local businesses.
words.example_zh: 犯罪分子试图向当地商家勒索钱财。

words.meaning_en: to say something in answer to a remark, typically in a sharp or witty way
words.derivation: re(回) + tort(扭) → 把话扭回去 → 反驳
words.example: She retorted that his argument was based on outdated evidence.
words.example_zh: 她反驳说他的论点基于过时的证据。

words.meaning_en: full of twists and turns; excessively lengthy and complex
words.derivation: tort(扭) + uous(…的) → 不断扭转的 → 曲折的
words.example: The tortuous mountain road made the journey slow and exhausting.
words.example_zh: 曲折的山路使旅途缓慢而疲惫。

words.meaning_en: severe physical or mental suffering; to cause to suffer
words.derivation: tor(扭) + ment(状态) → 被反复扭转的状态 → 折磨
words.example: The prisoner endured years of torment before being released.
words.example_zh: 那名囚犯忍受了多年的折磨才获释。

words.meaning_en: characterized by conflict, disorder, or confusion; not stable
words.derivation: turb(搅动) + ulent(…的) → 不断搅动的 → 动荡的
words.example: The country went through a turbulent period after the revolution.
words.example_zh: 该国在革命后经历了一段动荡的时期。

words.meaning_en: to make someone anxious or unsettled
words.derivation: per(彻底) + turb(搅动) → 彻底搅乱 → 使不安
words.example: The unexpected results of the experiment perturbed the researchers.
words.example_zh: 实验的意外结果令研究人员感到不安。

words.meaning_en: cloudy, opaque, or thick with suspended matter
words.derivation: turb(搅动) + id(…的) → 被搅动的 → 浑浊的
words.example: The river became turbid after days of heavy rainfall.
words.example_zh: 连日暴雨后河水变得浑浊不堪。

words.meaning_en: a state of great disturbance, confusion, or uncertainty
words.derivation: tur(搅动) + moil(劳作) → 不断搅动翻腾 → 混乱
words.example: The political turmoil led to widespread protests across the nation.
words.example_zh: 政治动荡导致了全国范围的抗议活动。

words.meaning_en: to come between so as to prevent or alter a result
words.derivation: inter(之间) + ven(来) → 来到中间 → 干预
words.example: The government decided to intervene in the economic crisis.
words.example_zh: 政府决定对经济危机进行干预。

words.meaning_en: to find a way around an obstacle or to overcome a problem
words.derivation: circum(周围) + vent(来) → 绕着来 → 规避
words.example: Companies sometimes try to circumvent environmental regulations.
words.example_zh: 企业有时会试图规避环境法规。

words.meaning_en: to come together for a meeting or activity
words.derivation: con(共同) + ven(来) → 一起来 → 召集
words.example: The committee will convene next Monday to discuss the proposal.
words.example_zh: 委员会将于下周一召开会议讨论该提案。

words.meaning_en: to violate or act against a law, rule, or agreement
words.derivation: contra(反对) + ven(来) → 反着来 → 违反
words.example: The new policy contravenes several international trade agreements.
words.example_zh: 新政策违反了多项国际贸易协定。

words.meaning_en: income, especially of a company or government
words.derivation: re(回) + ven(来) → 回来的东西 → 收入
words.example: The company's annual revenue exceeded two billion dollars.
words.example_zh: 该公司的年收入超过了二十亿美元。

words.meaning_en: to alternate or waver between different opinions or actions
words.derivation: vac(空) + illate(反复) → 内心空荡摇摆 → 犹豫不决
words.example: She vacillated between accepting the job offer and staying at her current position.
words.example_zh: 她在接受新工作和留在原岗位之间犹豫不决。

words.meaning_en: to remove people from a place of danger to a safer place
words.derivation: e(出) + vacu(空) + ate(使) → 使变空 → 疏散
words.example: Residents were evacuated before the hurricane made landfall.
words.example_zh: 飓风登陆前居民已被疏散。

words.meaning_en: having or showing a lack of thought or intelligence; empty
words.derivation: vacu(空) + ous(…的) → 空的 → 空洞无物的
words.example: The politician's vacuous promises failed to convince the voters.
words.example_zh: 那位政客空洞的承诺未能说服选民。

words.breakdown: e-(出) + van-(空) + -escent(逐渐变化后缀)
words.meaning_en: soon passing out of sight, memory, or existence; quickly fading
words.derivation: e(出) + van(空) + escent(逐渐) → 逐渐变空消失 → 转瞬即逝的
words.example: The beauty of the sunset was evanescent, lasting only a few minutes.
words.example_zh: 日落的美丽转瞬即逝，只持续了几分钟。

words.meaning_en: relating to heat
words.derivation: therm(热) + al(…的) → 与热有关的
words.example: Thermal energy from the sun can be converted into electricity.
words.example_zh: 来自太阳的热能可以转化为电能。

words.breakdown: geo-(地球) + therm-(热) + -al(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: relating to or produced by the internal heat of the earth
words.derivation: geo(地球) + therm(热) + al(…的) → 地球内部热量的 → 地热的
words.example: Iceland relies heavily on geothermal energy to heat buildings.
words.example_zh: 冰岛大量依靠地热能为建筑供暖。

words.meaning_en: an instrument for measuring temperature
words.derivation: thermo(热) + meter(测量) → 测量热度的工具 → 温度计
words.example: The thermometer indicated a temperature well below freezing.
words.example_zh: 温度计显示温度远低于冰点。

words.meaning_en: a device that automatically regulates temperature
words.derivation: thermo(热) + stat(稳定) → 使温度稳定的装置 → 恒温器
words.example: Adjusting the thermostat can significantly reduce energy consumption.
words.example_zh: 调节恒温器可以显著降低能耗。

words.meaning_en: relating to or in the form of words
words.derivation: verb(词语) + al(…的) → 用词语的 → 口头的
words.example: They reached a verbal agreement before signing the contract.
words.example_zh: 他们在签订合同前达成了口头协议。

words.meaning_en: using more words than needed; wordy
words.derivation: verb(词语) + ose(充满) → 充满词语的 → 冗长的
words.example: The professor's verbose lectures often ran over the allotted time.
words.example_zh: 教授冗长的讲座经常超出规定时间。

words.meaning_en: a short, well-known saying that states a general truth or piece of advice
words.derivation: pro(代替) + verb(词语) → 代替长篇大论的话 → 谚语
words.example: As the proverb says, 'Actions speak louder than words.'
words.example_zh: 正如谚语所说，「行动胜于雄辩」。

words.meaning_en: capable of working successfully; feasible
words.derivation: via(路) + able(能…的) → 有路可走的 → 可行的
words.example: Solar energy is becoming a more viable alternative to fossil fuels.
words.example_zh: 太阳能正成为化石燃料的一种更可行的替代方案。

words.meaning_en: to depart from an established course or norm
words.derivation: de(偏离) + vi(路) + ate(做) → 偏离道路 → 背离
words.example: The experiment's results deviated significantly from the hypothesis.
words.example_zh: 实验结果与假设产生了显著的偏离。

words.breakdown: de-(偏离) + vi-(路) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: showing a skillful use of underhanded tactics; not straightforward
words.derivation: de(偏离) + vi(路) + ous(…的) → 偏离正道的 → 狡猾的
words.example: The politician used devious methods to discredit his opponents.
words.example_zh: 那位政客用狡猾的手段诋毁对手。

words.meaning_en: keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties
words.derivation: vigil(警醒/守夜) + ant(…的) → 保持警醒的 → 警觉的
words.example: Citizens must remain vigilant against threats to public safety.
words.example_zh: 市民必须对公共安全威胁保持警惕。

words.meaning_en: physical strength and good health; effort and enthusiasm
words.derivation: vig(力量) + or(状态) → 充满力量的状态 → 活力
words.example: She pursued her research with remarkable vigor and determination.
words.example_zh: 她以非凡的活力和决心从事研究工作。

words.breakdown: in-(进入) + vigor-(力量) + -ate(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to give strength or energy to
words.derivation: in(进入) + vigor(力量) + ate(使) → 注入力量 → 使充满活力
words.example: The cool morning air invigorated the hikers as they set out.
words.example_zh: 清晨凉爽的空气让出发的徒步者精神焕发。

words.meaning_en: strong, healthy, and full of energy
words.meaning_zh: 充满活力的，精力充沛的
words.derivation: vigor(力量) + ous(…的) → 充满力量的 → 精力充沛的
words.example: The team launched a vigorous campaign to raise awareness about climate change.
words.example_zh: 该团队发起了一场大力宣传气候变化意识的活动。

words.breakdown: xeno-(外来的) + phob-(恐惧) + -ia(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: dislike of or prejudice against people from other countries
words.derivation: xeno(外来的) + phob(恐惧) + ia(症状) → 对外来者的恐惧 → 仇外
words.example: Xenophobia often increases during periods of economic instability.
words.example_zh: 仇外心理往往在经济不稳定时期加剧。

words.meaning_en: a person who is attracted to foreign peoples, cultures, or customs
words.derivation: xeno(外来的) + phil(喜爱) → 喜爱外来事物的人 → 亲外者
words.example: As a xenophile, she traveled extensively and learned five languages.
words.example_zh: 作为一个崇洋者，她游历广泛并学会了五种语言。

words.breakdown: xeno-(外来的) + gen-(产生) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: originating from outside the organism or system
words.derivation: xeno(外来的) + gen(产生) + ous(…的) → 由外部因素产生的 → 外源的
words.example: The infection was caused by a xenogenous pathogen introduced through contaminated water.
words.example_zh: 感染是由通过受污染的水引入的外源病原体引起的。

words.meaning_en: an act, statement, or gift intended to show gratitude or respect
words.derivation: trib(给予) + ute → 给出的东西 → 贡品/致敬
words.example: The concert was held as a tribute to the legendary musician.
words.example_zh: 这场音乐会是为了向这位传奇音乐家致敬而举办的。

words.meaning_en: punishment inflicted as vengeance for a wrong or criminal act
words.derivation: re(回) + trib(给予) + ution(行为) → 回报给予 → 报应
words.example: The villain feared retribution for his crimes against the community.
words.example_zh: 那个恶棍害怕因对社区犯下的罪行而遭到报应。

words.meaning_en: to regard something as being caused by; a quality or feature
words.derivation: at(朝向) + trib(给予) + ute → 把功劳给予某方向 → 归因于
words.example: Scientists attribute the warming trend to increased greenhouse gas emissions.
words.example_zh: 科学家将变暖趋势归因于温室气体排放的增加。

words.meaning_en: a cause of great trouble or suffering
words.derivation: tribul(压榨) + ation(状态) → 被压迫的状态 → 苦难
words.example: Despite many tribulations, the community rebuilt after the disaster.
words.example_zh: 尽管经历了许多磨难，社区在灾难后重建了。

words.meaning_en: a system of government in which priests or religious leaders rule in the name of God
words.derivation: theo(神) + cracy(统治) → 以神的名义统治 → 神权政治
words.example: In a theocracy, religious law serves as the basis for civil governance.
words.example_zh: 在神权政治中，宗教法是民事治理的基础。

words.meaning_en: a person who does not believe in the existence of God or gods
words.derivation: a(无) + the(神) + ist(人) → 不信神的人 → 无神论者
words.example: The philosopher identified as an atheist and based his ethics on reason alone.
words.example_zh: 这位哲学家自称无神论者，仅以理性为其伦理基础。

words.meaning_en: all the gods of a people or religion collectively; a temple dedicated to all gods
words.derivation: pan(全部) + theo(神) → 所有的神 → 万神殿
words.example: The Pantheon in Rome was originally built as a temple to all the gods.
words.example_zh: 罗马的万神殿最初是作为供奉所有神灵的寺庙而建造的。

words.meaning_en: the study of the nature of God and religious belief
words.derivation: theo(神) + logy(学说) → 关于神的学问 → 神学
words.example: She earned a degree in theology before entering the ministry.
words.example_zh: 她在进入教会工作前获得了神学学位。

words.meaning_en: reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little
words.derivation: tacit(沉默) + urn(倾向) → 倾向沉默的 → 寡言的
words.example: The taciturn professor rarely spoke outside of lectures.
words.example_zh: 这位沉默寡言的教授很少在课堂之外说话。
words.mnemonic: tacit(默契的/无声的)+urn→天生沉默寡言的人；联想：tacit approval = 默许

words.meaning_en: understood or implied without being stated
words.derivation: tacit(沉默) → 不用说出来就能明白的 → 默契的
words.example: There was a tacit agreement among the members not to discuss the issue publicly.
words.example_zh: 成员之间有一种默契，不公开讨论这个问题。
words.mnemonic: tacit谐音「他嘁」→他嘁了一声什么也没说→心照不宣

words.breakdown: re-(回) + tic-(沉默) + -ent(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily
words.derivation: re(回) + tic(沉默) + ent(…的) → 退回到沉默中 → 少言寡语的
words.example: He was reticent about his childhood experiences.
words.example_zh: 他对自己的童年经历讳莫如深。
words.mnemonic: reti(退)+cent(一分钱)→一分钱都不愿意说出口→沉默不言

words.meaning_en: to crush or squeeze something with force; to suppress
words.derivation: squ(挤压) + ash(重击) → 用力压 → 压碎/镇压
words.example: The government moved quickly to squash the rebellion.
words.example_zh: 政府迅速采取行动镇压了叛乱。
words.mnemonic: -ash系列重击词：squash/quash/smash/crash/clash/bash，都含猛烈撞击之感

words.meaning_en: to reject or void, especially by legal procedure; to suppress forcefully
words.derivation: 源自拉丁语quassare(打碎) → 强力压碎 → 撤销/镇压
words.example: The court decided to quash the conviction due to insufficient evidence.
words.example_zh: 法院因证据不足决定撤销定罪。
words.mnemonic: quash和squash同源：qu(力量)+ash(重击)→彻底压碎→撤销判决

words.meaning_en: to embarrass or make ashamed
words.derivation: a(使) + bash(打击) → 打击面子 → 使窘迫
words.example: Nothing seemed to abash the confident young speaker.
words.example_zh: 似乎没有什么能让这位自信的年轻演讲者感到窘迫。
words.mnemonic: a+bash(猛击)→给人一记当头棒喝→使人尴尬羞愧

words.meaning_en: not flowing or moving; showing no activity or development
words.derivation: stagn(池塘/停滞) + ant(…的) → 像死水一样不流动的 → 停滞的
words.example: The economy has been stagnant for the past three years.
words.example_zh: 过去三年经济一直停滞不前。
words.mnemonic: stag(雄鹿)+nant→雄鹿站在那里一动不动→停滞的；sta-系列静止词

words.meaning_en: very loyal and committed in attitude; to stop the flow of
words.derivation: sta(站立) + unch → 站定不动的 → 坚定的
words.example: She was a staunch supporter of environmental protection policies.
words.example_zh: 她是环保政策的坚定支持者。
words.mnemonic: staunch谐音「石桩似」→像石桩一样站立不动→坚定的

words.meaning_en: to prevent or hinder the progress of
words.derivation: 源自高尔夫术语：球被挡住无法推进 → 阻碍
words.example: Lack of funding stymied the research project for months.
words.example_zh: 缺乏资金使研究项目停滞了数月。
words.mnemonic: sty(猪圈)+mie(迷)→在猪圈里迷了路→被阻碍，无法前进

words.meaning_en: a written law passed by a legislative body
words.derivation: stat(确立) + ute → 确立下来的东西 → 法令
words.example: The statute prohibits discrimination based on race or gender.
words.example_zh: 该法令禁止基于种族或性别的歧视。
words.mnemonic: stat(站立/确立)+ute→被确立下来站住脚的规定→法令

words.meaning_en: great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause or objective
words.derivation: 源自希腊语zelos(热情) → 对事业的强烈热情
words.example: The volunteers worked with great zeal to rebuild the damaged community center.
words.example_zh: 志愿者们满怀热忱地重建受损的社区中心。
words.mnemonic: zeal谐音「自由」→为了自由充满热情→热忱

words.meaning_en: having or showing great energy or enthusiasm for a cause
words.derivation: zeal(热情) + ous(…的) → 充满热情的 → 狂热的
words.example: The zealous advocate fought tirelessly for human rights reform.
words.example_zh: 这位热情的倡导者不知疲倦地为人权改革而奋斗。
words.mnemonic: 注意与jealous(嫉妒的)区分：z→热情(zeal)，j→嫉妒(jealous)

words.meaning_en: a person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of ideals
words.derivation: zeal(热情) + ot(人) → 热情过头的人 → 狂热者
words.example: The political zealot refused to consider any opposing viewpoints.
words.example_zh: 这个政治狂热者拒绝考虑任何反对意见。
words.mnemonic: zealot=zeal+ot(人)→热情的人，过度热情→狂热分子

words.meaning_en: the highest point reached; the peak
words.derivation: 源自阿拉伯语samt ar-ra's(头顶上方) → 天顶 → 巅峰
words.example: The Roman Empire reached its zenith during the second century.
words.example_zh: 罗马帝国在二世纪达到了巅峰。
words.mnemonic: zenith中有zen(禅)→禅修达到最高境界→顶点

words.meaning_en: the lowest point in the fortunes of a person or organization
words.derivation: 源自阿拉伯语naẓīr(对面) → 与天顶相对的最低点 → 最低谷
words.example: The country's economy reached its nadir during the Great Depression.
words.example_zh: 该国经济在大萧条时期跌至最低谷。
words.mnemonic: nadir与zenith互为反义：nadir谐音「那底儿」→最底部→最低点

words.meaning_en: the top or highest part of something; the peak
words.derivation: 源自拉丁语apex(尖端) → 最高点
words.example: She considered winning the Nobel Prize the apex of her career.
words.example_zh: 她认为获得诺贝尔奖是她事业的巅峰。
words.mnemonic: apex中有ape(猿猴)→猴子爬到最高处→顶点；与zenith同义

words.meaning_en: the highest or climactic point of something
words.derivation: culmin(顶峰) + ation(过程) → 达到顶峰的过程 → 高潮
words.example: The discovery was the culmination of decades of scientific research.
words.example_zh: 这一发现是数十年科学研究的高潮。
words.mnemonic: culmination含culmin(顶峰)→攀登到顶点→事件的高潮

words.meaning_en: to take a position of power illegally or by force
words.derivation: usu(使用) + rp(夺取) → 非法使用权力 → 篡夺
words.example: The general attempted to usurp the president's authority through a military coup.
words.example_zh: 将军试图通过军事政变篡夺总统的权力。
words.mnemonic: usurp谐音「you蛇rp」→蛇篡夺了统治权→篡位；联想usury(高利贷)→非法牟利

words.meaning_en: a document authorizing police to make an arrest or search; to justify or necessitate
words.derivation: 源自古法语warant(保护者/保证) → 官方保证 → 授权令
words.example: The severity of the situation warrants immediate action.
words.example_zh: 形势的严峻性足以证明必须立即采取行动。
words.mnemonic: warrant=warr(war战争)+ant→打仗需要授权令→搜查令；warranty(保修)同源

words.meaning_en: present, appearing, or found everywhere
words.derivation: 源自拉丁语ubique(到处) + ous(…的) → 到处都有的 → 无处不在的
words.example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern society.
words.example_zh: 智能手机在现代社会已无处不在。
words.mnemonic: ubi(哪里)+quit(离开)+ous→哪里都不离开→无处不在

words.meaning_en: a poisonous substance secreted by animals; extreme malice or spite
words.derivation: 源自拉丁语venenum(毒药/爱情魔药) → 毒液/恶意
words.example: The snake's venom can be fatal if left untreated.
words.example_zh: 如果不加以治疗，这种蛇的毒液可能是致命的。
words.mnemonic: venom=ven(维纳斯Venus的爱)+om→爱之毒药→毒液；恋爱像中毒

words.meaning_en: to make drunk or to excite or exhilarate beyond self-control
words.derivation: in(进入) + toxic(毒) + ate(使) → 使毒素进入体内 → 使中毒/使陶醉
words.example: The audience was intoxicated by the singer's powerful performance.
words.example_zh: 观众被歌手强大的表演力所陶醉。
words.mnemonic: in(进入)+toxic(有毒的)+ate→让毒进入体内→中毒→陶醉(像中了毒一样)

words.meaning_en: to remove toxic substances from
words.derivation: de(去除) + toxic(毒) + fy(使) → 使去除毒素 → 解毒
words.example: The liver plays a crucial role in detoxifying harmful substances in the body.
words.example_zh: 肝脏在排除体内有害物质方面起着至关重要的作用。
words.mnemonic: de(去除)+toxify(使有毒)→去掉毒性→解毒
